races.race_name: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Kodiak Breeze
runners.spotlight: Clear of the rest when second to the promising Arapaho Gold on his 5f course debut and made no mistake over 6f at Redcar (good) 16 days later, making all; wandered around under pressure at Redcar but was well on top and should improve again.
runners.comment: Second on course debut before comfortably making all over 6f at Redcar; could be useful

runners.name: Possessive
runners.spotlight: Flashed his tail but stuck on to prevail by 1l in novice event at York (6f, good) on debut; only seventh of 13 when sent off 5-2 favourite for the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) 12 days later but likely to have a big say back in calmer waters.
runners.comment: York debut win; beaten favourite in Woodcote at Epsom next time but this is easier

runners.name: Location Location
runners.spotlight: Failed to take a step forward from his AW debut when beaten over 6l into third at Hamilton (6f, good) 45 days ago but remains open to progress; hooded first time.
runners.comment: Has shown ability both starts and more to come at some point; hooded first time

runners.name: Whisper Angel
runners.spotlight: 32,000euros half-sister to winners Feyha (1m2f AW 2yo/1m4f; RPR 71), Martinus (German 7f) and Corazon Blanco (Czech 1m1f); her stable has a couple of useful 2yos this year so she's worth a second look in the betting.
runners.comment: Worth a second look in betting for yard which has two useful juveniles this year

runners.name: Desert Outlaw
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros yearling, resold for 5,000euros; gelding by Arizona; fifth foal; brother to 7f/1m AW winner Glyndwr (including 2yo; RPR 86) and half-brother to two Italian winners; best to look elsewhere on debut unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Best to look elsewhere on debut unless the market speaks favourably

runners.name: Terryfying
runners.spotlight: Gelded son of Dream Ahead who went through the ring for 1,000gns as a foal; a watching brief is advised on debut unless the betting strongly hints otherwise.
runners.comment: Watching brief advised on debut unless betting hints otherwise

runners.name: Dontlookanyfurther
runners.spotlight: £35,000 breeze-up 2yo who is the third foal of an unraced half-sister to Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint winner Starlust; the stable has yet to have a 2yo winner this season but he still needs monitoring in the betting.
runners.comment: Stable yet to have a 2yo winner this season but he still needs monitoring in the betting

runners.name: Mr Resdev
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Diligent Resdev (6f/7f including 2yo; RPR 92) and Nammos (7f AW/1m turf; 75); best watched on debut unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Half-brother to useful winner in these silks but watching brief is still advised on debut

runners.name: Market Equities
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to five winners, including Third Time Lucky (7f-1m1f; RPR 108) and Baba O'Riley (French 6f-1m including 2yo; 96); would enter the reckoning if the market speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: Newcomer who would enter the reckoning if the market speaks in his favour
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Loveulongtime
runners.spotlight: Weakened quickly to finish remote last of seven on 7f Wetherby debut 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Weakened quickly to finish remote last of seven on 7f Wetherby debut 16 days ago

runners.name: Court Legend
runners.spotlight: 80-1, always behind in the Rous seller at York (6f, good) on debut 19 days ago.
runners.comment: 80-1, always behind in 6f York seller on debut

runners.name: Neverbeatdeswalker
runners.spotlight: 50-1, weakened to finish a remote last of seven on York debut (6f, good) 18 days ago.
runners.comment: 50-1, weakened to finish remote last of seven on York debut (6f, good) 18 days ago

races.race_name: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Arapaho Gold
runners.spotlight: Pulled 2.5l clear of a next-time-out winner Kodiak Breeze (runs in first division) when overcoming inexperience to make a very promising winning debut over 5f here (good to firm) at the start of May; it's taken a bit of time to get him back to the track but 6f should suit even better and he looks more than good enough to defy a penalty.
runners.comment: Promising winning start to career over 5f here two month ago; up to defying penalty

runners.name: Brixworth Bandit
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on debut when fourth of seven over 5f at Carlisle (good) 33 days ago but he was beaten just under 6l and will likely need to improve a good bit again to seriously trouble Arapaho Gold.
runners.comment: Stepped up on debut when fourth at Carlisle but more needed again to go close

runners.name: Travellers Girl
runners.spotlight: Made some late headway when fourth of seven on Ripon debut (6f, good) a fortnight ago; entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Some late headway when fourth of seven on 6f Ripon debut a fortnight ago; may do better

runners.name: Noccalula Falls
runners.spotlight: 5,000 euros yearling; sister to 1m winner Alabama Calling (RPR 82) and half-sister to four other winners; stable hardly noted for first-time-out juvenile success.
runners.comment: Newcomer from yard hardly noted for first-time-out 2yo success

runners.name: That's All Right
runners.spotlight: £1,000 yearling who is the first foal of a mare who was placed 7f-1m (RPR 76); most from the stable are better for a run but the betting should provide more clues.
runners.comment: Most from yard are better for a run but betting should provide more clues

runners.name: Mangham
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when last of eight on Carlisle debut (5f, good to soft) 22 days ago.
runners.comment: 28-1 when last of eight on Carlisle debut (5f, good to soft) 22 days ago

runners.name: Harry Who
runners.spotlight: Space Blues colt who is a half-brother to a couple of very useful winners abroad; needs a betting check for a stable which has had a couple of first-time-out 2yo winners this year.
runners.comment: Some useful winners in his pedigree and needs checking out in betting
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Scarlet Dynasty
runners.spotlight: 9,000euros yearling closely related to 7f AW winner River Chorus (RPR 70) and half-brother to winners Sioux Spirit (5f 2yo/6f; 90) and Biloxi Boy (5f 2yo; 76); dam 12.5f winner (including AW; 73); the betting should guide to expectations on debut.
runners.comment: The betting should help guide to expectations with this newcomer

runners.name: Mick's The Boy
runners.spotlight: Showed pace before fading in well-contested York seller on debut (6f, good to firm) 19 days ago; may last longer this time.
runners.comment: Showed pace in strong York seller on debut; may lost longer this time

runners.name: Roman Crystal
runners.spotlight: Finished over 8l behind the reopposing Travellers Girl on her 6f Ripon debut two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Finished over 8l behind the reopposing Travellers Girl on 6f Ripon debut two weeks ago

runners.name: Claret Time
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and hooded, hinted at ability amidst greenness when fifth of nine on 6f Redcar debut (good) 11 days ago; may do better.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability amidst greenness on debut; may do better

races.race_name: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fozzie
runners.spotlight: Showed fairly useful form when 66-1 second on 7f course reappearance but hasn't quite reproduced that level in his three outings, not helping his cause with some increasingly headstrong tendencies; needs to settle better if he's to go the right way.
runners.comment: Good second on 7f course return but hasn't matched that since, pulling too hard last twice

runners.name: North Force
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful form when placed on first four starts, notably second of nine on 7.5f Beverley handicap debut (good to soft) in May; his run of consistent efforts came to an abrupt halt at Nottingham last time and cheekpieces are reached for on the back of that; shouldn't be far away if back to his best but still likely vulnerable to an improver.
runners.comment: Run of consistent form came to abrupt halt latest and cheekpieces go on now

runners.name: Lord d'Or
runners.spotlight: His dam was a German 1m Group 3 winner; left summer's AW debut well behind when second to the promising Portcullis in 1m York novice (good) on reappearance in May, keeping on in a style which suggests he's sure to win races.
runners.comment: Promising second in 1m York novice on reappearance and looks sure to win races
runners.owner: RC Syndicate X and Gary Gillies

runners.name: Prince Of Calypso
runners.spotlight: Third on both starts nine months apart (gelded in between), actually bettering the form of his debut in a ten-runner race when last of three at Nottingham (extended 1m, soft) three weeks ago; cheekpieces worn there are left off now.
runners.comment: Fair form when third on both starts nine months apart; cheekpieces worn latest off now

runners.name: Woheela
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling; fourth foal; sister to 7f winner Rajala (RPR 75), half-sister to winners Vento D'Estate (Italian 7.5f-8.5f) and Ikigai Star (2m/2m3f hurdle); dam 7.5f 2yo Listed/1m Italian 1,000 Guineas winner (102); tongue tied for debut but Tom Marquand booked and she needs checking out in the betting.
runners.comment: Tongue tied but this 150,000gns yearling needs checking out in betting with Marquand up

runners.name: Steel Raven
runners.spotlight: It took this 87,000euros 2yo breeze-up buy a while to get to the track but he looks capable of making up for lost time after a promising opening run at Carlisle (9f, soft) 22 days ago, finishing well from the rear for a never-nearer third of 12; that doesn't obviously point to this sharper test suiting but he could just be a lot more clued up second time out.
runners.comment: Promise when running on for never-nearer third on 9f Carlisle debut; surefire improver

runners.name: Brouhaha
runners.spotlight: In good hands but no promise when remote sixth over 1m on AW and 7f on good to firm this spring.
runners.comment: Remote sixth on both starts and needs significant improvement to get heavily involved

runners.name: Fair Isle Sioux
runners.spotlight: 6yo mare who hinted at ability on her 6f Redcar debut in May 2024 but not seen again until trailing home at Carlisle (1m, soft) 23 days ago.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability on 2024 but not seen again until trailing home at Carlisle 23 days ago

runners.name: Moral Victory
runners.spotlight: 150-1 when tailed off on recent Southwell debut.
runners.comment: 150-1 when tailed off on recent Southwell debut

races.race_name: Army Benevolent Fund Handicap

runners.name: Mister Sky Blue
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name from 21 attempts but he has finished second in two of his three runs this year and is still on a workable mark; new blinkers could spark something extra (ran well in one attempt in cheekpieces) and he's more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but arrives in form and the new blinkers could give him a lift

runners.name: Ellie's de Vega
runners.spotlight: Consistent enough this year but he will need to raise his game a notch if he is to come out on top in this field.
runners.comment: Consistent enough this year but he needs to raise his game a notch to take this

runners.name: Bishop's Glory
runners.spotlight: 6f AW win in November (visored first time) and placed on a few occasions subsequently; neither start since returning from a four-month break has been near that level though.
runners.comment: Chance on this winter's AW form but well short of his best in two runs last month

runners.name: Desert Master
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner but 0-14 on turf; his last two runs on grass have offered hope that he can win a small race on fast ground though and he is one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Three AW wins; 0-14 on turf but his last two runs offer hope he can put that right soon
runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner

runners.name: Dolly's Delight
runners.spotlight: Ran well on her stable debut at Wolverhampton last October but only run to that level once from four starts this year; returns to sprinting with the tongue-tie discarded; dangerous mark but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Not his best form so far this year; drops back in trip with the tongue-tie discarded
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Petes Diamond
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all ten starts; whiff of promise at Nottingham in April but he is opposable on balance.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all ten starts and not easily recommended

runners.name: Night Emperor
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for Hugo Palmer but his two turf runs for this yard in May were both encouraging; edging down the weights and he should appreciate a strong pace at this trip.
runners.comment: Two turf runs for this yard in May were encouraging enough; still has low mileage

runners.name: Thehunnebelllegacy
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but she has snippets of form that give her a squeak; last week's fifth of seven at Catterick wasn't as bad a run as the distance beaten suggests (hampered at a crucial stage) and she's not out of it.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who had an excuse at Catterick last week; not out of it
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: The Floors Munky
runners.spotlight: No improvement for handicapping (6f, good and 1m, soft) in May; tongue-tie and cheekpieces added but he has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicaps in 2 runs in May; addition of accessories not enough to tempt

runners.name: Barmyblade
runners.spotlight: Won three sprint handicaps last spring and he is now 9lb lower than for the last of them; 2026 had been a struggle until he emerged from the doldrums with a good second at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago; now has something to build on.
runners.comment: On good mark and returned to form with recent 2nd at Ripon; now has something to build on

runners.name: Indy's Angel
runners.spotlight: 6f AW winner as a 2yo but her losing run stands at 15 and this year's efforts don't suggest it will be ending any time soon.
runners.comment: 6f AW win as a 2yo; this year's efforts haven't pointed to her adding to that success soon

runners.name: Hyrcanian
runners.spotlight: Regressive 17-race maiden; too much to prove for comfort.
runners.comment: Regressive 17-race maiden; too much to prove for comfort

runners.name: Stormy Pearl
runners.spotlight: She has run some good races in big-field handicaps over C&D and this is her level; not far off her recent best at Carlisle 16 days ago; each-way shout if getting it right at the start.
runners.comment: Has run well in big-field C&D handicaps in the past; e-w shout if getting the start right

runners.name: Uncle Sam
runners.spotlight: 6f AW winner for Kevin Ryan; second of ten in a 5f classified event at Southwell on his seasonal/stable debut 13 days ago; no problem with conditions and he's one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Promising second in 5f AW classified event 13 days ago; effective over C&D; shortlisted

runners.name: Everydaysasaturday
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all eight starts; got a bit closer to the target when fifth of 12 in a 5f handicap at Newcastle six weeks ago (blinkered first time); suspicion he'll do better at some stage but he is not the obvious answer this time.
runners.comment: Unplaced all 8 starts; bit better tried blinkered 6 weeks ago (5f) but progress essential

runners.name: Up The Monk
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins over this trip but he wasn't at his best when last seen in March; his three turf starts are all excusable to some degree and he could prove as effective on grass in time.
runners.comment: Two AW wins; yet to shine on turf but there have been mitigating factors

runners.name: Mrs Trump
runners.spotlight: Won twice last July, including when beating 17 rivals in the corresponding race (good ground; 2lb lower today); arrived in better form last year but she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Won this race off 2lb higher last year but arrived in better form; revival not beyond her
runners.owner: R Chapman, G J Daly & R J Parks

runners.name: Hurstwood
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose by Mrs Trump in this race last year when rated 5lb higher; not at his best this year but his Redcar fourth in May offered hope he could add to his 11 wins.
runners.comment: Beaten a nose by Mrs Trump in this race last year when rated 5lb higher; could revive
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Golden Prosperity
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last summer; landed a 5f handicap at Catterick off a 2lb higher mark in April but he hasn't come close to replicating it in six runs since; capable if breaking on terms but he carries some risk.
runners.comment: C&D winner; chance on April's Catterick win but he has been a shade disappointing since

races.race_name: David Lever Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Liverpool Star
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 11th attempt when gamely landing a 6f handicap at Ripon (good ground) in April; beaten at short odds when fourth to Simba's Pride at Hamilton shortly afterwards and his form then dipped at Pontefract (5f) four weeks ago; returning to 6f should help; cheekpieces worn last time are quickly discarded.
runners.comment: 6f win at Ripon in April; not so good twice since and he needs to up his game

runners.name: Hares Bredth
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt with a ready win at Redcar (6f, good) 11 days ago, strong at the finish; up 5lb in a higher grade but he's not fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Strong at finish when winning at Redcar 11 days ago; up 5lb in a better race but unexposed

runners.name: Concert
runners.spotlight: Reportedly unsuited by the ground at Bath on her second run this year but that is sandwiched by solid efforts at Wolverhampton and Newcastle; capable of a prominent showing.
runners.comment: Two good AW runs this year and she won't be far away with a repeat

runners.name: Saucy Jane
runners.spotlight: Won a Beverley maiden on her second start but 0-12 since; more realistically handicapped again now and she ran a sound race at Pontefract (6f, good; cheekpieces first time) last month; soft ground excuses latest lesser effort; not discounted down into a 0-70.
runners.comment: Ground excuse last time and previous Pontefract second was more like it; not discounted
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners

runners.name: Go Lockers Go
runners.spotlight: Started his 3yo campaign with a 6f win at Chelmsford in March and he followed up at Yarmouth (5f, good) one month later; beaten favourite but performed with credit at Nottingham (5f) and Wolverhampton (6f) since; sneaking up the weights but likely to give another good account.
runners.comment: Two wins and two seconds from four runs this year; another solid effort seems likely
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing D Turner B Roberts

runners.name: Showtown
runners.spotlight: Yet to win but he has finished second three times in novice/maiden company; cheekpieces have been worn for his three handicap runs and he has finished unplaced, albeit a bit of mid-race interference didn't help his cause over C&D four weeks ago; now drops into a 0-70 and could yet come good.
runners.comment: Not matched maiden/novice form in handicaps; eased in grade; still has time to come good

runners.name: Simba's Pride
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 6f handicaps in May; beaten favourite in 5f handicaps at York and Thirsk on his last two starts but has left the impression there is still a bit more to come; return to 6f will help and he's a major player.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins in May; beaten fav in 5f handicaps on last two runs; more to come back at 6f

runners.name: King's Road Dandy
runners.spotlight: Promise in his sole 2yo start (6f, good to firm); no progress in two runs this year but he has been sent off a single-figure price on each start and could take a step forward now handicapping at a realistic level.
runners.comment: Not built on his 2yo debut promise in two runs this year; may do so now handicapping

runners.name: Celtic Arrow
runners.spotlight: Creditable efforts in three Newcastle maidens in February/March (6f twice and 5f); while she still has time to do better, that AW form is not strong and improvement is essential.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in three Newcastle maidens in February/March; this demands more

runners.name: Houndhill
runners.spotlight: The addition of a visor saw him break his duck in a 5f handicap at Newcastle in May; always on the back foot after an awkward start at York 19 days ago; this is weaker and he should be fine back at 6f but others appeal as being better treated.
runners.comment: 5f AW win in May (visored first time); awkward start did him no favours last time
runners.owner: J Raybould N Skinner D Jenkins

runners.name: Masai Angel
runners.spotlight: His two runs this year have both threatened better and this well-bred 3yo moves into handicaps with some potential; he could do with settling better if he is to fulfil it though.
runners.comment: Bred to be useful and has shown promise in 6f maidens this year; needs to settle better

runners.name: Country Artiste
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and she has yet to shine for her new stable, beaten 14l behind Simba's Pride at Ripon five weeks ago; lots to prove.
runners.comment: 0-10 and not shone for new yard, beaten 14l behind Simba's Pride at Ripon on latest

races.race_name: Weatherbys Racing Bank Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: York Tower
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for a switch to handicaps when fourth of nine at Epsom (1m2f, soft) on Derby day but stuck on in a style which suggests this step up to 1m4f could unlock some improvement; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Staying-on fourth on 1m2f Epsom handicap debut suggests 1m2f could unlock improvement

runners.name: Temple
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third on 1m4f Newmarket handicap debut (good) 12 days ago, staying on in a style which suggests there could be more to come as his stamina is drawn out.
runners.comment: Best effort when third on 1m43f handicap debut latest; more to come as stamina drawn out

runners.name: Parisian Scholar
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who landed three 1m handicaps last autumn, the last two at Newcastle (Tapeta); twice placed at Kempton (1m3f, Polytrack) earlier this year but recent efforts less encouraging.
runners.comment: Three 1m wins last autumn; placed over 1m3f this year but recent efforts less encouraging

runners.name: Asia Force
runners.spotlight: Consistent form when runner-up in all three novice events and third of nine on handicap debut at Chester (1m2f, good to soft); rallied last time as if 1m4f could help.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three novices and creditable third on 1m2f handicap debut; 1m4f could suit

runners.name: Yafreh
runners.spotlight: Brother to 7f-1m2f winner Al Mubhir (RPR 115); has made steady progress in his three starts, finishing 4l second to a useful prospect in 1m3f Hamilton maiden (good to soft) three weeks ago; makes handicap debut; should continue to improve.
runners.comment: Promise in his three qualifying runs and likely more to come in handicaps

races.race_name: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Handicap

runners.name: American Style
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on 2025 best; ran a fine race for third on his seasonal return at Ripon in April but two heavy defeats have followed (behind Novello Lad latest); needs a full revival.
runners.comment: On a good mark but he needs to bounce back from two heavy defeats

runners.name: Indian Run
runners.spotlight: Dropped into Class 3 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 16 days ago and ran a good race for second, edging left in the closing stages but keeping on well regardless; still has handicapping scope up 1lb.
runners.comment: Good second at Windsor last month; up 1lb but still has handicapping scope; contender

runners.name: Novello Lad
runners.spotlight: Progressive sprinter in 2025 (following wind surgery) and he has carried on the good work this year, winning 6f handicaps at Doncaster in May (good to soft) and June (good to firm); up 4lb in a stronger race but he may not have finished progressing just yet.
runners.comment: Progressive sprinter; chasing hat-trick after two Doncaster wins this summer; up in class

runners.name: Juan Les Pins
runners.spotlight: He had dropped a long way in the weights before ending a losing run at Newcastle (5f) in March; added a C&D win in April and he has run well in defeat in both runs since; creeping back up the weights and had a 53-day break but he has the ability to go well.
runners.comment: Enjoying a revival this year, including a C&D win; should give another good account

runners.name: Mister Sox
runners.spotlight: He has run well over C&D in the past, including in May, and he didn't run badly when third of six in a Class 3 handicap at Pontefract (6f, good) ten days ago; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Has C&D form and he is handicapped to go well; each-way shout

runners.name: Veblen Good
runners.spotlight: Got up late to win a 6f handicap at Pontefract on his stable debut in April; no impact in a 7f handicap at Epsom on Oaks day; others bring stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Winning stable debut at Pontefract in April; well short of that level over 7f latest

runners.name: Lord Roxby
runners.spotlight: Back to form with a bang when seeing off 19 rivals at York (6f, good) in May; similar form when fourth of 21 back at York 18 days ago; should remain competitive.
runners.comment: A win and a 4th in big-field York handicaps on last two starts; should remain competitive
runners.owner: N Skinner D Jenkins J Glover

runners.name: Strong Warrior
runners.spotlight: Won his first two starts in maiden/novice company; second of nine at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on his handicap debut last June but it has been tougher going since; latest third at Ripon was a promising enough return to action though and he should give a good account.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap but latest Ripon third was encouraging; should give a good account

runners.name: Bergerac
runners.spotlight: Fine servant down the years but he hasn't won over 6f since August 2022; on a good mark and can pop up soon but would like to see more from him before getting involved.
runners.comment: Nearly four years since last 6f win; not shone in two runs this year; risks attached

runners.name: Westmorian
runners.spotlight: 6-21 on AW but still seeking a first turf win after 14 attempts; ran okay at York last month without suggesting he was the answer today.
runners.comment: 6-21 on AW but 0-14 on turf; ran okay at York latest without suggesting he was the answer

races.race_name: William Hill Summer Cup Saturday 1st August Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: He has run a string of good races in 2026, winning for the second time when comfortably seeing off five rivals at Beverley (7.4f, good) last week; that effort was backed up by the Topspeed figure and he can't be discounted under a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Stayed on strongly to win 7.4f handicap at Beverley last week; contender under 5lb penalty

runners.name: Al Muqdad
runners.spotlight: Placed in fast-ground 7f handicaps at Ayr and Doncaster on his last two starts, missing out by a nose on Friday; another big run on the cards with a talented apprentice taking off 5lb.
runners.comment: Two good runs in 7f turf handicaps this summer, missing out by a nose last week; solid

runners.name: In A Hurry
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden over C&D last summer; back to form with a front-running second of seven in a fillies' handicap at Catterick (7f, good to firm; tongue tied first time) nine days ago; on a dangerous mark and one of two solid contenders for David O'Meara.
runners.comment: C&D winner; back to form with front-running 2nd at Catterick 9 days ago; trainer runs two

runners.name: Singoura
runners.spotlight: Still to add to her 2yo novice win; finished second of eight at Redcar (7f, good) in May but not in the same form in either run since; opposable.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap and she hasn't been at her best on her last two starts; opposable

runners.name: We've Got This
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Redcar (good to firm) last summer off 2lb lower; not at her best over 1m this year; cheekpieces discarded today.
runners.comment: 7f win at Redcar last summer; not at her best over 1m this year; cheekpieces discarded

runners.name: Avatar Jet
runners.spotlight: Front-running success at Southwell (1m) in April; two sound efforts have followed but he will need more for the drop back to 7f if he is to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Front-running 1m AW win in April; beaten off this mark twice since; now drops back in trip

runners.name: Vince Le Prince
runners.spotlight: 7lb below his last winning mark but that was back in October 2024; retains ability and he has been placed in all three C&D runs; should give his running.
runners.comment: On a losing run but he has been placed in all three of his C&D runs; should go well again

runners.name: Rory's Gold
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; solid effort over 1m at Ayr (soft) 12 days ago but that was a Class 6 and he is facing different conditions here.
runners.comment: Good second at Ayr 12 days ago but over further and on soft ground

runners.name: Farandaway
runners.spotlight: It took him 17 attempts to put his head in front, finally opening his account in a 6f maiden at Newcastle in March; solid second in a 6f Southwell handicap one month later; absent for 81 days and although he isn't on a bad mark, he does have to prove his stamina for 7f.
runners.comment: In good form on AW earlier in the year; stamina to prove back on turf after a break

runners.name: Men Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden; ran his best race for new connections when staying on over 6f at Doncaster 11 days ago; stays 7f but he may need a drop into Class 6 to break his duck.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden; latest Doncaster run (6f) was better but may need a drop in class
runners.owner: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Imperial Guard
runners.spotlight: Over two years since his sole success and well held in two starts this year; on a dangerous mark but that has been the case for some time; returning to 7f not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: On a long losing run and yet to shine this year; return to 7f not enough to tempt

runners.name: King Of War
runners.spotlight: Returned from seven months off with a good third at Brighton (1m, good) last month; made all for an easy success back at Brighton (7f, good to firm) nine days ago but set strong pace when disappointing there (1m) on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Easy Brighton win nine days ago; set strong pace when disappointing there (1m) on Tuesday

runners.name: Way To Dubai
runners.spotlight: Low-key run at Newmarket (6f, good) 12 days ago; return to a longer trip is in his favour but others are more appealing for win purposes.
runners.comment: Returning to 7f can help but others look more appealing for win purposes

runners.name: War Howl
runners.spotlight: Won 7f handicaps at Doncaster and Ascot last summer (trained by Mick Appleby) and he is thrown in on that form; big prices and no impact in six runs for new connections but he now drops into a Class 5 and a market check is essential.
runners.comment: Thrown in on last year's turf form; no impact for new yard but a market check is essential
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

races.race_name: Perrigo Consultant Tax Saving Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Atreides
runners.spotlight: Consistent either side of two wins at about 2m4f last December (good to soft and heavy) but there's nothing eyecatching about his mark now and he needs to find extra for the new trip.
runners.comment: Consistent either side of two 2m4f wins last December; needs extra for new trip

runners.name: Betty's Tiara
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on debut in 2023; finally off the mark over hurdles (2m7f, good) last September but she's run to an ordinary level back in handicaps on last two starts and goes chasing with something to prove.
runners.comment: Finally off the mark over hurdles last September; modest handicap form since; chase debut

runners.name: Axel Bleue
runners.spotlight: Irish point win in 2025 was followed by two bumper successes in Britain; failed to win over hurdles since though and he hasn't looked about to score over fences of late either; probably needs more weight off his back.
runners.comment: Won Irish point/two bumpers in Britain in 2025; maiden over hurdles; well held over fences

runners.name: Duel Au Soleil
runners.spotlight: Successful handicap hurdle debut at Wetherby (about 2m5f, good) last October; no match at all for the winner on chase debut at Bangor (about 2m4f, good) in April but the return to this longer trip can help; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces; yard runs two.
runners.comment: Won handicap hurdle in October; creditable 3rd on chase debut; return to 2m7f can help

runners.name: Yes Day
runners.spotlight: Career has had its ups and downs but he won two chases (3m1f and 3m6f) in March 2025 and ran one of his better races of the year when second of five at Stratford (3m4f, good to firm; blinkers replaced cheekpieces) in May; capable of going well over this lesser test if holding his form.
runners.comment: Career had ups and downs but ran well latest and capable of going well if holding his form

runners.name: Gwennie May Jem
runners.spotlight: Three chase wins since April 2025, at 2m and 2m4f, but he was pulled up in two of his last three starts for Olly Murphy and has a stamina query over the new trip on first run since January.
runners.comment: Three chase wins since April 2025; pulled up in January; new trip on yard debut

runners.name: Quick Sharpener
runners.spotlight: Had a spell in the doldrums for previous yard, so was well treated on debut for this one at Hereford (2m5f, good) in May when having a match in safe keeping from a fair way out; that form amounted to little (second well below best then and since) but this one has gone back up just 2lb and should stay this first attempt beyond 2m5f under rules.
runners.comment: Won a match on yard debut in May; still well treated on old form and should stay 2m7f

runners.name: Pike Road
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins in 2025, at about 3m on good and soft, and his recent second here was a return to form; has lower chase mark but hard to assess back over fences, having weakened on chase debut then unseated rider three out at Ludlow (still led sole remaining rival) that month; shade to prove.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins at about 3m; back to form latest; shade to prove back over fences

runners.name: I Am The Moon
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins, most recently in February, but she's been out of luck in three chase runs, including when a remote last of four at Uttoxeter (about 3m, good) in June; has it to prove for now.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins but out of luck in three chase runs, including latest (about 3m)

runners.name: Gwash
runners.spotlight: Won maiden hurdle in 2024; best effort for a long time when third of 11 here (2m7f, good; closely matched with Pike Road) in June but he was beaten too far in one previous chase start under rules, at Taunton last November, to have confidence now back over fences.
runners.comment: Ran well over hurdles latest but poor effort in one previous chase run, last November

runners.name: Doyouknowwhatimean
runners.spotlight: Has had some modest results since going close on yard debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, heavy) before Christmas; others much higher up the list.
runners.comment: Modest form since going close on yard debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, heavy) before Christmas

races.race_name: Brookes Wedding Feast & Fair Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Culligran
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; tenacious winner of 2m5f chase at Newton Abbot (good) last June but soft ground may have counted against her next time and she wasn't seen after July; a long way back on her recent yard debut but there's still hope for her at this level on anything like her old form.
runners.comment: Tenacious winner of 2m5f chase last June; not seen after July; long way back on yard debut

runners.name: Majestic Moment
runners.spotlight: Useful chase strike-rate, winning three of six completions (at up to about 2m3f; good to soft and good) since October; well below best over C&D latest but overall form suggests he could soon put that behind him.
runners.comment: Won 3 of 6 chase completions since October; way below his best in June; possible reviver

runners.name: Knightsbridge
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler who is already doing better over fences, having stayed on well to win at Warwick (3m, good) in June on his second chase start; had run well over a similar trip to today's the time before and, although back up 5lb, he should have more to come.
runners.comment: Ran well on chase debut (2m4f); stayed on well to win over 3m latest; back up 5lb; improve

runners.name: King Of The Story
runners.spotlight: Four chase wins include two here, one in this race last year; creditable return from a break at Fontwell in April but he's struggled in two Warwick runs since, the step up to 3m seemingly not the main issue last time; just below his latest winning mark though so hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Below best of late but won this last year and just below his most recent winning mark

runners.name: Pawpaw
runners.spotlight: Four chase wins at 2m5f to 3m2f, most recently at Hereford (soft) in February 2025; has been off since though and past record suggests he's unlikely to be tuned up for this; goes without regular cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Four chase wins at 2m5f-3m2f, latest in early 2025; off since; no great record when fresh

runners.name: Jullou De Grissay
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner here (2m7f, good) last August for previous yard but he's had a rocky ride for this one, including on last two chase starts (the latest following wind surgery); hard to be confident of a revival.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner here (2m7f) last August; suffered heavy defeats on last 2 chase starts
runners.owner: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green

runners.name: Calli Black
runners.spotlight: Looked a potential improver when convincing winner on second chase start, over C&D (good) last June; hasn't delivered on that promise since and was right off her game at Ffos Las in April; at least this is easier.
runners.comment: Looked to have chase potential when C&D winner last June but has much to prove these days

runners.name: Crackerjack Queen
runners.spotlight: Very modest hurdle form but she went chasing off a career-low mark at Southwell (2m4f, good) in June and took a healthy step forward, keeping on well against one in a good run of form; back up 2lb; can threaten.
runners.comment: Very modest hurdler but took step forward when 2nd on chase debut (2m4f); can threaten

runners.name: Emberscombe
runners.spotlight: Better known as a pointer, adding two more in the spring, and her challenge didn't last long over C&D three weeks ago; up against it from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Better known as a pointer; well beaten back under rules over C&D in June; out of weights

runners.name: Tech That
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise in a bumper and over hurdles but he's been pulled up on his last two starts, over hurdles and on chase debut, and returns from his second wind surgery since the autumn with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Has ability but pulled up on last two starts, chase debut latest; 2 wind ops since autumn

races.race_name: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Neigh Botha
runners.spotlight: Made light of a five-month absence when gaining a third chasing win in his last four runs in 2m Warwick handicap in May; up 5lb but not taken lightly.
runners.comment: Gained third chasing win in last four runs at Warwick in May; up 5lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: Greer Hill
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in a handful of runs over hurdles but this Irish point runner-up was much improved on his chasing debut when a 28-1 winner of nine-runner handicap over C&D two weeks ago; up 5lb but open to progress, so he's a player.
runners.comment: Much improved when making winning chasing debut over C&D; up 5lb but a player

runners.name: Turpin Gold
runners.spotlight: Much improved for Tom Lacey last season when a three-time 2m hurdles winner; off six months and made a promising start in this sphere with third of five in 2m1f handicap at Stratford 32 days ago, running on late under a patient ride; one to be interested in off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler; promising chasing start with running-on Stratford third; interesting

runners.name: Tommy Cullen
runners.spotlight: Gained a first chasing success at Doncaster (2m) last February; infrequently raced since and he came in a well-held fourth of five in 2m5f Fakenham handicap 31 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth of five in Fakenham h'cap 31 days ago; needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Jack To Bat
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden hurdler; yet to offer much in three runs sent chasing though, undergoing wind surgery before pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase here 25 days ago; blinkers and tongue strap go on here.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much in three runs sent chasing, pulled up here latest; blinkered/tongue tied
runners.owner: Martyn & Elaine Booth & The Maiden Over

runners.name: Miss Denver
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler; yet to offer much in two runs in this sphere, hooded when a well-held third of four in 2m4f handicap here 25 days ago; lots more is required from 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Hooded when well-held third of four here 25 days ago; lots more is required

runners.name: Lord Chamberlain
runners.spotlight: Fair winning 2m hurdler who wasn't discredited when fifth in 2m5f handicap at Market Rasen in May; much respected now going chasing.
runners.comment: Not discredited when fifth over hurdles at Market Rasen in May; much respected now chasing

runners.name: Disco Davis
runners.spotlight: Ended his time over hurdles for Dan Skelton under something of a cloud; not disgraced on yard/chasing debut with third in 2m4f handicap here but pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase back here 20 days ago; has something to prove now.
runners.comment: Pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase here 20 days ago; has something to prove now

runners.name: Merely A Detail
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper winner; yet to add to that over hurdles but she has made the frame on all her six runs, a non-staying fourth in 2m4f Fakenham novice in May on latest; needs considering back in trip for her chasing debut.
runners.comment: Non-staying hurdles fourth at Fakenham latest; considered back in trip on chasing debut

runners.name: Stormin Crossgales
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Charles and Adam Pogson with 2m5f Fakenham chasing win last May; has failed to complete on all his five runs for his current stable however, tried in cheekpieces when pulled up at Leicester (2m4f) in February last time.
runners.comment: Failed to complete all five runs for current stable, pulled up in February last time

races.race_name: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Trouville
runners.spotlight: Quite useful on the Flat for the Crisfords over 1m4f on turf and at staying trips on AW; returned from a year off with an encouraging hurdle debut over C&D (good) in June for new yard, settling well and mostly jumping soundly behind two who had won on the Flat in France; looks sure to improve.
runners.comment: Quite useful on Flat; off for a year; encouraging 3rd on hurdle debut over C&D

runners.name: Hawk's Rock
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a Galway bumper; has run with credit against some good prospects in first two hurdle starts at Downpatrick and Cartmel (both 2m6f on good) in May but he was struck into when pulled up at Southwell since; has a part to play if this much shorter trip doesn't count against him.
runners.comment: Creditable shows in first two hurdle races; struck into when pulled up since; first 2m run

runners.name: Fine Fellow
runners.spotlight: Early promise in a bumper in May 2025; has hinted at some ability over hurdles and this is easier than when a remote fourth over C&D in June but he will be eligible for handicaps after today.
runners.comment: Early promise in a bumper; well held over hurdles; future looks to lie in handicaps

runners.name: Versatile
runners.spotlight: Flat winner at 1m2f in May 2025; refused to race in May this year but he came nearest to a first hurdle win when close up in an ordinary handicap at Southwell (2m, good) three weeks ago; shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Came nearest to first hurdle win when close 3rd in ordinary 2m handicap last month

runners.name: Diamond White
runners.spotlight: Placed on good to soft in all three bumper starts but she dropped out after making the early running on hurdle debut at Fontwell (about 2m1f, good; 16-1) in May; handicaps look more likely than a maiden win.
runners.comment: Placed in all three bumpers; dropped right out on hurdle debut at Fontwell (16-1)

runners.name: Do Wah Diddy
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in the first of three bumpers, at Huntingdon in November 2024; looked to need the run when down the field at Bangor in May following an 18-month absence; may well need this hurdle debut, too.
runners.comment: Minor bumper promise in late 2024; well beaten in May on return from absence; hurdle debut

runners.name: Jorah d'Alma
runners.spotlight: Returned from two years off to win a Ludlow bumper in 2025; up against it on first two hurdle starts but he ran better when third of eight finishers at Bangor (about 2m3f, good) in March and wasn't far away, though held, when falling at the last at Warwick since; in contention.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; some hurdle promise on last 2 starts (fell latest); should be in contention

runners.name: Faithful Guardian
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to Group 3 and Listed winners; has run respectably in two of three bumpers, most recently behind two newcomers here in May on yard debut; others may be too sharp for him now he's hurdling.
runners.comment: Respectable bumper form, latest 3rd here in May on yard debut; others may be too sharp

runners.name: Show Your Hand
runners.spotlight: Bred to come into his own over further than 2m but he's shown plenty of pace at Exeter and Uttoxeter (both on good) in his last two bumpers, getting worn down only late on; likely contender now hurdling.
runners.comment: Has shown plenty of pace in last two bumpers, worn down late on each time; hurdle debut

runners.name: Luckonthemoor
runners.spotlight: 300-1, pulled up on belated debut over C&D last month.
runners.comment: 300-1, pulled up on belated debut over C&D last month

runners.name: Bacardi Blue
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in a bumper, two points and a maiden and a novice hurdle (80-1 and 200-1).
runners.comment: Beaten long way in a bumper, two points and a maiden and a novice hurdle (80-1 and 200-1)
runners.owner: D Summersby J May W Dodd

races.race_name: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Drombeg Lad
runners.spotlight: Nearly won a bumper here last summer and later close up in two runs over hurdles; no improvement when beaten 10l here 20 days ago but he did race freely after a break; entitled to be in the thick of it.
runners.comment: Has the form to be on the thick of it and he was quite free here last time after a break

runners.name: Purple Owl
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer; runner-up for a second time over hurdles (0-6) when rallying over C&D 20 days ago; none too consistent and may ideally prefer further, but he boasts some of the best form among these.
runners.comment: May ideally prefer further but he boasts some of the best form among these

runners.name: Earnest Belief
runners.spotlight: 0-6 on Flat for the Gosdens; not beaten all that far in two of his three races over hurdles, latterly at Bangor in this new headgear combination; should have some role to play.
runners.comment: 0-6 on Flat for Gosdens; not beaten all that far in two of his three races over hurdles

runners.name: Sword Of Wessex
runners.spotlight: 1-14 on the Flat, the win coming over about 1m3f at Yarmouth (good; fair form) in April; beaten 14l into second in a six-runner maiden hurdle at Huntingdon a month ago; likely to need more here.
runners.comment: Fair Flat winner; beaten 14l into second in a six-runner maiden hurdle at Huntingdon

runners.name: Dunstall Star
runners.spotlight: The winner of a weak good-ground bumper at Exeter; raced freely in his two runs over hurdles; weakening when falling in the first but then finished clear of the third behind a long odds-on winner at Warwick (2m, good) and that's something to build on.
runners.comment: Small-field bumper winner; clear second behind the hot favourite on second hurdle run

runners.name: Delgany Second Now
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to high-class jumper Any Second Now; beaten 20l in a bumper and even further in a maiden hurdle here two weeks ago when again easy to back; in a top yard but has no obvious form claims and looks one for further down the line.
runners.comment: In a top yard but has no obvious form claims and looks one for further down the line

runners.name: Giant's Way
runners.spotlight: Closely related to high-class 2m hurdler Labaik; had wind surgery prior to running in a bumper here when he faded late on (wore a tongue-tie); sent off one of the favourites that day and should be capable of better.
runners.comment: Faded into fifth in a bumper here but he did go off one of the favourites; may do better

runners.name: Emmas Wish
runners.spotlight: The winner of two from 11 points; can only be watched on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: The winner of two from 11 points

runners.name: Walkingtheline
runners.spotlight: Minor form under rules for Tom Lacey; has since gone 2-7 in points; others are much preferred.
runners.comment: Minor form under rules for Tom Lacey; has since gone 2-7 in points

runners.name: He's Tailor Made
runners.spotlight: Raced freely when tailed off in his first bumper; hooded for the second at Hereford (2m, good) and stayed on encouragingly from well off the pace to be about 15l off the winner; an unlikely winner of this but might be one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Shaped better in his second bumper when ridden from well off the pace in first-time hood

runners.name: Scoro
runners.spotlight: Only 1-12 in points and 50-1 when tailed off on hurdle debut at Uttoxeter.
runners.comment: Only 1-12 in points and 50-1 when tailed off on hurdle debut at Uttoxeter

races.race_name: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Newport
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort who gained a deserved success in conditional jockeys' handicap at Hexham (2m) ten days ago; escapes a penalty for that ready success but he badly ran out of steam once turning for home at Stratford (2m3f) 7.48 yesterday.
runners.comment: Ready success at Hexham ten days ago; badly ran out of steam at Stratford (2m3f) yesterday

runners.name: Mojo Ego
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 but he signed off for James Moffatt with a fair sixth of 13 in 2m1f Cartmel handicap in May; not out of things for his new handler.
runners.comment: Signed off for James Moffatt with fair Cartmel sixth in May; not out of it for new yard

runners.name: Al Mootamarid
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m2f scorer on the Flat who got off the mark in this sphere in 2m Stratford handicap last June; back from ten months off with a promising third of eight over C&D two weeks ago, finishing well having suffered a poor run from two out; very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Promising C&D third latest, finishing well after a poor run; very much one to consider

runners.name: Both Of Us
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m Flat winner for George Boughey; in the frame on all his four runs since sent hurdling, undergoing wind surgery before a solid fourth of eight on his handicap debut over C&D two weeks ago; considered.
runners.comment: Had a wind op before solid fourth on his handicap debut over C&D two weeks ago; considered
runners.owner: Mrs H Watson & Mrs Carol Lee

runners.name: Phantom Gold
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper winner for Ben Brookhouse; brought along steadily for current yard before much improved on his handicap debut with second of 11 at Southwell (2m) 22 days ago, running on strongly late on having had a lot to do; big player with better to come.
runners.comment: Much improved with running-on second at Southwell latest; big player with better to come

runners.name: Belcamo
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win in this sphere in five-runner handicap at Fakenham (2m) when last seen in January; up 3lb but has gone well fresh, so not ruled out after a break.
runners.comment: First hurdles win at Fakenham in January; has gone well fresh, so not ruled out

runners.name: Jet Patrol
runners.spotlight: Quick to make his mark for James Owen with a pair of 2m handicap hurdle wins at Stratford and Plumpton last summer; off the track since a remote sixth at Warwick (2m) seven months ago though, so needs to hit the ground running again.
runners.comment: Dual winner for James Owen; off since remote sixth at Warwick seven months ago though

runners.name: Castle Quarter
runners.spotlight: Scored at Uttoxeter (2m) last May but this veteran has not proved easy to catch right since, last of ten on the Flat at Salisbury 17 days ago.
runners.comment: Scored at Uttoxeter last May but this veteran has not proved easy to catch right since
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs R Newiss & Gavin Teversham

runners.name: Blazing Saint
runners.spotlight: 33-1 but easily best run on his first go in handicaps when landing 13-runner event at Southwell (2m) in May; up 4lb but this lightly raced 5yo is open to further progress.
runners.comment: Easily best run when landing 2m Southwell handicap last time; open to further progress

runners.name: Uncle Remus
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful bumper scorer last July; yet to match that form in five runs over hurdles, going too freely after six months off when ninth of 12 in 2m Ffos Las handicap in May; should strip sharper here.
runners.comment: Fairly useful bumper scorer last July; yet to match that form in five runs over hurdles

races.race_name: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ballylicky Bay
runners.spotlight: Point/bumper winner; might be finding her feet over hurdles having stayed on encouragingly for third here in May and she looks ready for this longer distance.
runners.comment: Only her second handicap when third here in May and she looks ready for this longer trip

runners.name: Barafundle Bay
runners.spotlight: 1m3f winner two years ago; 0-4 over hurdles and pretty much tailed off in two handicaps (2m4f/2m); has an awful lot to prove.
runners.comment: 0-4 over hurdles and pretty much tailed off in two handicaps (2m4f/2m)

runners.name: Magna Patch
runners.spotlight: Modest profile under rules but handicaps were always going to be more like it and he needs checking in the betting; raised in distance.
runners.comment: Handicaps were always going to be more like it and he needs checking in the betting

runners.name: Roland Garros
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 6 handicap in April (1m4f, good) but tailed off in his next Flat race and suffered the same fate back over hurdles at Uttoxeter (first-time blinkers now removed); looks risky.
runners.comment: Flat winner in April but nothing since under either code

runners.name: Karthala
runners.spotlight: Third in both his handicaps and finished with a rattle in the latest at Perth (2m, good); on the back of that and raised in distance, he's firmly in the reckoning.
runners.comment: Third in both his handicaps and finished with a rattle in the latest at Perth (2m, good)

runners.name: Scoresby
runners.spotlight: Multiple point winner who has raced just four times over hurdles and won a 2m3f Fontwell handicap in May (good); disappointing off this 5lb higher mark next time but that looked too bad to be true.
runners.comment: Fontwell winner; the following run at Newton Abbot was perhaps too bad to be true

runners.name: Khalk'eau Spigao
runners.spotlight: Showed a tad more when beaten about 12l in his first handicap (2m3f, good; 33-1) but was still only fifth of the eight runners; dropped 4lb.
runners.comment: Beaten 12l in his first handicap but only fifth of the eight runners; dropped 4lb
runners.owner: D S Dennis & John Hw Finch

runners.name: Dynamite Defense
runners.spotlight: Placed in low-grade AW handicaps for Olly Murphy earlier this year; beaten 20l over hurdles on yard debut and then unseated in his first chase; risky.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l over hurdles on yard debut and then unseated in his first chase; risky

runners.name: Portetta
runners.spotlight: 0-2 in bumpers and 0-7 over hurdles; 20-1 when never a factor in a C&D handicap 20 days ago.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles; 20-1 when never a factor in a C&D handicap 20 days ago

runners.name: Stavanger Rose
runners.spotlight: Ropey form figures for previous yard and well behind in two runs for this one; tackles her first handicap but with much to prove.
runners.comment: Tackles her first handicap but with much to prove

runners.name: Locked Down Lad
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer with placed form over hurdles but he's now 0-14 and his runs earlier this year were poor.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles but now 0-14 and his runs earlier this year were poor

races.race_name: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Iamyouare
runners.spotlight: Gained a first handicap success at Hereford (2m4f) in April but seemed unsuited by dropping back to 2m1f when third of five in novice at Cartmel in May; this daughter of Jukebox Jury remains one to be interested in over this much longer trip.
runners.comment: Unsuited by drop to 2m1f with Cartmel third in May; one to be interested back up in trip

runners.name: Artiste d'Ainay
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer; gained a first success under rules on just his second run for current yard in 3m Southwell handicap hurdle 22 days ago; up 7lb but not taken lightly.
runners.comment: First success under rules at Southwell 22 days ago; up 7lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: Touchwoodexpress
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Newton Abbot (3m2f) last September but below par all three subsequent runs, beating one in a 3m2f Warwick handicap four weeks ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways at Newton Abbot last September but below par all three runs since

runners.name: Masterdream
runners.spotlight: A three-time chase winner (at up to 3m2f) last season; pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase here in May, so has it to prove on his first run in this sphere since April 2024.
runners.comment: Pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase here in May; has it to prove back in this sphere now

runners.name: Barry Fife
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden hurdler; failed to kick on sent handicapping when only fifth of seven at Uttoxeter (2m7f) in May; remains with few miles on the clock, though, so not written off.
runners.comment: Only fifth at Uttoxeter in May; remains with few miles on the clock, so not written off

runners.name: Pure Seduction
runners.spotlight: Got off to a winning start over hurdles over 2m here in August 2024; a remote last to finish on all her three starts since, however, latest her chasing debut at Stratford (2m4f; three ran) in May; very hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Remote last to finish on last three starts, latest her chase debut; very hard to warm to

runners.name: Man Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m1f winning hurdler who wasn't disgraced when fourth on the Flat at Bath 18 days ago; this veteran is no forlorn hope back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when fourth on Flat at Bath 18 days ago; no forlorn hope back in this sphere

runners.name: Lacrima
runners.spotlight: Bagged a hat-trick over fences (up to 2m2f) in France last summer; yet to match that form for his new yard but better signs with fifth of 11 over C&D 25 days ago; not out of things if building on it.
runners.comment: Better signs for new yard with C&D fifth 25 days ago; not out of things if building on it

runners.name: Granite Sands
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third in 2m6f Stratford novice in May; came in a well-held fifth in similar company at Southwell (2m4f) 22 days ago but he may still do better now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Well-held fifth at Southwell 22 days ago; may still do better now going into handicaps

runners.name: Roxboro River
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but he got back on track with second of eight in 2m7f handicap at Bangor five days ago; now needs to back it up.
runners.comment: Back on track with second at Bangor five days ago; now needs to back it up

races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Scandi Noir
runners.spotlight: Outran 33-1 odds at Cork (6f, good) when third of 14, 2.25l behind Ballinea Star, who took Group 3 honours at the Curragh last weekend; huge player off the back of that despite carrying joint top-weight
runners.comment: Third of 14 and 2.25l behind subsequent Group 3 winner on debut; huge player

runners.name: Away And Gone
runners.spotlight: Didn't run to market expectations when favourite on debut at Cork (5f, soft); better effort at Navan (5f, good) when fourth of nine; this trip can unlock more from her based on her pedigree and she receives weight from a few.
runners.comment: Disappointed as fav' on debut but better effort when fourth latest; this trip may help

runners.name: Alwaysanangel
runners.spotlight: Bought for 75,000gns at breeze-up in April; sister to 6f/7f winner Yorkshire (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 104), half-sister to winners My Awele (5f/6f inc AW; 82), Amarachi (6f; 61) and Maritot (French 1m-1m2f); dam unraced half-sister to Italian 7.5f 2yo Listed winner Junia Tepzia; tongue tied for debut but of obvious interest if the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: 75,000gns breeze-up purchase in April; of obvious interest despite tongue tie

runners.name: Anglesea Bridge
runners.spotlight: Purchased for 27,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to French 8.5f AW winner Tuuli; dam 7f 2yo/1m2f winner (RPR 91), half-sister to useful winners Tamarillo (1m1f UAE Oaks), Take It To The Max (6f-1m2f) and Wake Up Call (7f), out of 14.7f winner; has the attributes on paper to be a player.
runners.comment: 27,000euros yearling purchase; dam 7f 2yo/1m2f winner (RPR 91); interesting on paper

runners.name: Ivory River
runners.spotlight: Last of 17 and 26l behind the winner over C&D on debut (good); hard to fancy off the back of that despite the addition of a visor.
runners.comment: Last of 17 and 26l behind the winner over C&D on debut; hard to fancy

runners.name: Crayons In The Sky
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly was bought for 43,000gns as a yearling and improved in value to 80,000euros at a breeze-up in May; half-sister to winners See That Storm (1m/1m2f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 103), Star Chorus (5f/6f inc AW; 92), Bell Song (6f-1m AW; 88) and Bright Summer (7f AW; 81); dam 5f/6f winner (inc 2yo/AW; 93); check market albeit concedes weight to most.
runners.comment: 80,000euros breeze-up buy; half-sister to four winners; check market

runners.name: Cyclone Code
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear colt was purchased for 34,000gns as a yearling; dam Listed placed over 5f as a 2yo; okay debut at Roscommon when a 6.5l sixth of ten (7.5f, good), weakening late on; not ruled out but others more appealing
runners.comment: Okay debut when a 6.5l sixth of ten; not ruled out but others more appealing

runners.name: Pink Venom
runners.spotlight: 13.5l behind in seventh at Leopardstown last month (7f, soft, 80-1); others more likely.
runners.comment: 13.5l behind in seventh at Leopardstown last month (7f, soft, 80-1); others more likely

runners.name: Arrowstock
runners.spotlight: Moderate debut when sixth of nine at Navan (5f, good, 50-1), 2.5l behind Away and Gone; kept on late that day, so this step up in trip can't hurt.
runners.comment: Moderate sixth of nine on debut; this step up in trip can't hurt

races.race_name: Dino Day At Fairyhouse On July 8th Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Mini Cotai
runners.spotlight: Best efforts this year have come over 5f on soft ground; stable enjoyed a major sprint win at the Irish Derby meeting on Sunday.
runners.comment: 14-race maiden, placed over 6f on AW/5f on turf, stable had a major sprint win on Sunday

runners.name: I'm Spartacus
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner; scored for the first time on turf here on penultimate start; has much the same chance as Rosato on running here just under three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Best known as a six-time AW winner, made turf breakthrough here on penultimate start

runners.name: Hero Of The Hour
runners.spotlight: Seven wins between turf and AW over 5f-6f for former yard; made a good start for this stable with C&D fourth; midfield last week in a Naas event in which Tawaazon was second.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner, made a good start for this stable with C&D fourth, midfield last week

runners.name: Step Back In Time
runners.spotlight: Course specialist at Navan, scene of all four wins; mid-division over C&D last time; held by several of these rivals on that evidence.
runners.comment: Course specialist at Navan, scene of all four wins, mid-division over C&D last time

runners.name: Jackandthefox
runners.spotlight: 22-race maiden; satisfactory run over C&D last month; satisfactory run over C&D but was behind Monastere and Livingston Range then
runners.comment: 22-race maiden, satisfactory C&D run latest but was behind Monastere and Livingston Range

runners.name: Love Bomb
runners.spotlight: Nothing of much note in her form in eight starts since a 5f Down Royal win last autumn; potential excuses at Gowran last time, raced too keenly and met some interference.
runners.comment: Nothing of much note in her form in eight starts since a 5f Down Royal win last autumn

runners.name: Whatswrongnow
runners.spotlight: 29-race maiden, uneven form this year but two of his better efforts have been over C&D; second in an 18-runner contest in May and third of 16 last month, with I'm Spartacus and Rosato among those behind.
runners.comment: 29-race maiden, uneven form this year but two of his better efforts have been over C&D

runners.name: Glen Breeze
runners.spotlight: C&D second early last month was encouraging; failed to reproduce that form a week later; new headgear combination now.
runners.comment: C&D second early last month was encouraging, failed to reproduce that form a week later

runners.name: Rosato
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner, below her best this term; however, she was well backed here last time and finished only one place behind I'm Spartacus, who had won here the previous week.
runners.comment: Three-time winner, below her best this term, one place behind I'm Spartacus last time

runners.name: Monastere
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; produced massive improvement when second at 66-1 over C&D last month on handicap debut; obvious chance now if building on that.
runners.comment: Produced massive improvement when second at 66-1 over C&D last time, obvious chance now

runners.name: She's On The Ball
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; towards the rear at Cork on handicap debut; took a step in the right direction when fifth at Limerick; well supported then; noteworthy.
runners.comment: Took a step in the right direction when fifth at Limerick, well supported then, noteworthy

runners.name: Tara Na Ri
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens at last autumn; achieved his best finishing position when fifth here last time; that was over 1m2f, and he may find this trip inadequate.
runners.comment: Achieved his best finishing position when fifth here last time but that was over 1m2f

runners.name: Lady Kelly
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden form; fair effort on handicap debut when sixth over 7f at this venue in first-time blinkers; led for most of the way when third over 7f at Down Royal 12 days ago; good chance now for in-form stable
runners.comment: Led for most of the way when third over 7f at Down Royal, good chance for in-form stable

runners.name: Run Forrest Run
runners.spotlight: 6-46 on turf; losing run stretches back to October 2024; poor form on AW last winter; shaped reasonably well at Listowel on first run for this stable; ran to roughly the same standard at Limerick; stablemate She's On The Ball is more appealing.
runners.comment: 6-46 on turf, last win was in October 2024, stable's She's On The Ball is more appealing

runners.name: Tawaazon
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner; returned to form when second of 21 over 5f at Naas last week; has tended to show his best form at this distance, though also effective over 5f/7f; holds a definite chance
runners.comment: Returned to form when second of 21 over 5f at Naas last week, holds a definite chance

runners.name: Clarabell
runners.spotlight: Never in the first six in ten outings between September 2024 and March 2025; best watched on first run for Ado McGuinness.
runners.comment: Never in the first six in ten outings, best watched on first run for this stable

runners.name: Livingston Range
runners.spotlight: Did not get the best of runs over 5f at Naas last week; previous fourth over C&D was respectable; needs to reverse form with Monastere.
runners.comment: Did not get the best of runs over 5f at Naas, previous fourth over C&D was respectable

runners.name: Rappell
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins, none since a Curragh success in 2024; also a C&D winner during the same campaign; no better than ninth in five outings since April; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Three turf wins, none since a Curragh success in 2024, recent form well below his best

races.race_name: Racing Again July 8th Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Imnotleavinyou
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three soft-ground handicaps from 6f-1m this year before a below-par run on quicker ground; won on yielding ground at Limerick; beaten favourite at Gowran two days later, possibly the run came too soon.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite at Gowran last time, that run may have come soon after a Limerick win

runners.name: Queen Aethelflaed
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key start to the season before hitting form last month; second over 1m at Gowran before winning over same course and trip; was carrying a 7lb penalty when fourth over 1m1f at Leopardstown last time.
runners.comment: Has achieved a good standard of form in her last three outings over longer trips than this

runners.name: Altimara
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back 7f wins at Leopardstown in August and placed twice over 1m subsequently; below best in two runs this season; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, won twice over 7f at Leopardstown last season, yet to hit form this term

runners.name: Minaun View
runners.spotlight: 0-18 record is off-putting despite the encouragement offered by a recent Down Royal second; on the positive side, she has been no worse than sixth in her last five races.
runners.comment: 0-18 record is off-putting despite the encouragement offered by a recent Down Royal second

runners.name: Bundle Of Interest
runners.spotlight: Won at Navan last July (6f, good); poor run over 1m at Dundalk in November on final start last year; needs to step up considerably from her seasonal debut at Naas; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, needs to step up considerably from her seasonal debut, fair form last year

runners.name: Mo Anam Chara
runners.spotlight: Two Dundalk runs last November/December were much better than previous turf efforts on soft ground; faded in the final furlong on handicap debut in a 1m contest at the Curragh; winning favourite in a Roscommon claimer; faces stronger opposition here
runners.comment: Winning favourite in a Roscommon claimer in May faces stronger opposition here

runners.name: Elly Bay
runners.spotlight: Solid placed efforts in maidens last year for Johnny Murtagh; C&D second in May her best handicap effort so far; first time with cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Fair maiden form for Johnny Murtagh, C&D second in May her best handicap effort so far

runners.name: Dervo Annie
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 6f before fair fifth over this trip at Down Royal; would merit consideration if making the line-up; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, course winner over 6f before fair fifth over this trip at Down Royal

runners.name: Two And Two
runners.spotlight: Sprang a 40-1 surprise when getting off the mark at Dundalk in January over 6f; solid Curragh handicap run next time but below that level in two outings last month; fourth of six over 5f at Down Royal on latest.
runners.comment: AW winner over 6f, needs to improve on turf form, fourth of six over 5f on latest

runners.name: Carrigans Grove
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a successful 2025, winning three times; yet to hit form this term; back on her last winning mark; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a fine spell in sprint handicaps last year, back on her last winning mark

runners.name: Caitouna
runners.spotlight: Two respectable Dundalk runs; fair effort at Leopardstown on turf debut; mid-division at the Curragh in her first handicap; Colin Keane aboard, but others have stronger credentials
runners.comment: Mid-division at the Curragh in her first handicap, others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Hasita
runners.spotlight: Contested two strong Curragh maidens last May/June; returned with never-nearer third of 15 at Cork (6f, soft); another solid display over this trip at Down Royal; makes handicap debut with a fair chance for in-form team.
runners.comment: Has shown useful form in maidens, makes handicap debut with a fair chance for in-form team
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Sagasti
runners.spotlight: Improved from first two outings to finish third in a 7f Dundalk maiden last December; was having only her second run in a handicap when third at Gowran last month; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Was having only her second run in a handicap when third at Gowran, open to improvement

runners.name: Bear Right
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame on her last two starts, both over 1m; does not need to improve much to feature here.
runners.comment: Has made the frame on her last two starts, does not need to improve much to feature here

runners.name: Jasmine Affanalis
runners.spotlight: Tipperary winner last summer was only just denied a second career win over this trip at Killarney in May; had no luck in running at Limerick last time; should not be judged harshly on that; cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Went very close at Killarney in May, no luck in running at Limerick last time, cheekpieces

runners.name: Wild Berries
runners.spotlight: Best of her three efforts in maidens was over this trip at Naas; well beaten over 1m in a nursery at the same venue; down the field on seasonal debut at the Curragh; not one of the more obvious contenders
runners.comment: Down the field on seasonal debut, not one of the more obvious contenders

runners.name: Little Miss P
runners.spotlight: Fair form in maidens last year; disappointing on handicap/seasonal debut at Leopardstown; dropped in trip after a poor run at Roscommon; plenty to prove at this stage.
runners.comment: Dropped in trip after a poor run at Roscommon, plenty to prove at this  stage

runners.name: Casla's Eagle
runners.spotlight: Handicap mark is based on three runs in quick succession in April/May; not easy to assess her chance, but others preferred.
runners.comment: Handicap mark is based on three runs in quick succession in April/May, others preferred

runners.name: Rodeeve
runners.spotlight: Bolted up over 6f here last month, beating Monastere who bids to advertise the form in the 5.40 event; should be fine with 7f; 12lb rise is challenging, same rider takes off 7lb again.
runners.comment: Bolted up over 6f here last month, should be fine with 7f but 12lb rise is challenging

runners.name: Are You In Or Out
runners.spotlight: Gained her second course 6f win on seasonal/stable debut; has a bit to find with Minaun View on Leopardstown running.
runners.comment: Gained her second course 6f win on seasonal/stable debut, behind Minaun View on latest

runners.name: Famed Again
runners.spotlight: Maiden had fair 7f form at two for Tom Cooper; lesser form for this yard, but not disgraced mid-field at Roscommon in May on seasonal debut; could improve.
runners.comment: Maiden had fair 7f form at two for Tom Cooper, not a bad run at Roscommon in May

races.race_name: Early Bird Winter Festival Tickets At Fairyhouse.ie Handicap

runners.name: Bella Colombia
runners.spotlight: Added a second AW win at Dundalk in March (1m); 0-14 on turf but has run some decent runs in defeat; last two runs at Limerick and Roscommon have been satisfactory; back in 0-60 company now; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner is 0-14 on turf but has run well in defeat; down in grade; shortlisted

runners.name: La Campanella
runners.spotlight: Has shown glimmers of ability at Dundalk and ran well for a long way back on turf here last time (1m2f, good), prominent turning in before fading; can be expected to improve over that seasonal return with cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long time here last time over 1m2f; expected to come on from that

runners.name: Launch Time
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough when landing a Leopardstown 1m handicap in April (good) off 49; ran to a similar level at Cork later in the month when beaten 3l into fourth; disappointing effort over 1m1f at Leopardstown last time when beaten 12.5l, but soft ground a possible excuse; Keane back aboard now.
runners.comment: Landed a big-field 1m h'cap in April off 49; sft a possible excuse last time; interesting

runners.name: William F Browne
runners.spotlight: Two Flat wins came in 2024; two hurdle runs didn't go to plan but bounced back with a good third over 1m1f back on the level at Leopardstown (good to firm), headed inside the final 110yards; this trip looks ideal for him and a tongue tie is added to the cheekpieces; in the mix off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Good third over 1m1f last time, headed late on; this is his ideal trip; chance.

runners.name: Milverton
runners.spotlight: Yet to make an impact in nine Flat handicaps; sixth over 1m2f here but tailed off at Down Royal next time; unlikely to be the answer.
