runners.comment: Yet to make an impact in nine h'caps and tailed off last time; unlikely to be the answer

runners.name: Believeth
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in maidens and never in contention in two handicap starts; drop of 3lb further not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in m'dens nor in two h'caps; drop of 3lb more not enough to tempt

runners.name: Heart Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 6f AW maiden in 2024; 0-12 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; one of her better efforts when 6l behind the winner over 1m on soft at Limerick last time; dropped 3lb.
runners.comment: AW m'den winner in 2024; yet to trouble the judge in 12 turf runs.
runners.owner: B O'Sullivan/D Whyte/Exors of the Late D McHugh/M O'Loghlen

runners.name: Rushford
runners.spotlight: No win since 2022; finished last in two Dundalk runs back from a long absence and was again last back on turf here in June; impossible to fancy.
runners.comment: No win since 2022 and impossible to fancy on form.

runners.name: Pete's Dream
runners.spotlight: Maiden from 13 starts but creditable seasonal return at Down Royal (7f, soft) when fourth of 11; could improve with that run under his belt.
runners.comment: 0-13 but creditable seasonal return when fourth at Down Royal; could improve

runners.name: Famous Enough
runners.spotlight: Won a Cork 1m handicap last season off 40 (good) and added an AW win in September; went close off 2lb higher at Dundalk in April; subsequent two turf runs have been disappointing and his record of 1-21 on this surface is concerning.
runners.comment: Won at Cork last season off 40 but 1-21 overall record on turf is concerning

runners.name: Pure Valour
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; chance is not obvious, perhaps tongue-tie will spark improvement, but others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Weak m'den form; chance is not obvious but perhaps tongue tie can spark something
runners.owner: Mrs Annette Coogan & J M O'Reilly & Frank Condra

runners.name: Blue Anthem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (the most recent of which was off a mark of 70); six outings for new yard have been disappointing including when dropped into 0-60 company for the first time at Roscommon on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Three-time British winner; disappointing for new yard, including at Roscommon yesterday

runners.name: Tullyesker
runners.spotlight: Moderate efforts in maidens; tongue-tied for handicap debut but may need this seasonal return.
runners.comment: Moderate efforts in m'dens; tongue-tied for h'cap bow but may need this return

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Vanir
runners.spotlight: Improved from mid-division Curragh debut (1m2f, good) when second over a slightly shorter trip at Gowran; arguably not sure to stay as well as some of these on pedigree; stable's current good form is a positive.
runners.comment: Promising second at Gowran, not sure to stay 1m4f. yard's current good form is a positive

runners.name: Pleased
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; half-sister by Camelot to a 7f Group 3; dam 5f/6f winner (including three Listed races); dam's side is speedy, but her two races have been over this trip; encouraging Limerick debut in April, took third behind an odds-on stablemate; still looked a bit raw when second (again behind a shorter-priced stablemate) at the Curragh last month.
runners.comment: Has run creditably twice as stable's second-string, good chance of a breakthrough win here

runners.name: Cosmetic
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly is closely related to Group 1 winners Hydrangea, The United States and Hermosa; superbly-bred but has looked limited in four runs; beaten 11l in fifth in Dundalk AW fillies' maiden in April; got closer when taking the same place over 1m4f at Cork in May.
runners.comment: Frankel filly with a superb pedigree, has looked limited so far but unwise to rule out
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Essential Comedy
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on recent debut at Leopardstown; plenty of improvement required.
runners.comment: Showed some ability on recent debut, plenty of improvement needed, positive jockey booking

runners.name: Spry
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; Jim Bolger-bred half-sister by Teofilo to winners Sometimesadiamond (6f inc 2yo/Listed), Level Pitch (7f-1m2f ) and Speiriuil (hurdles); dam a middle-distance winner by Galileo; tongue-tie for debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Teofilo to three winners, dam a middle-distance winner by Galileo

runners.name: French Blond
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always behind on debut at Naas; unlikely to play a featuring part.
runners.comment: Slowly away and always behind on debut at Naas, unlikely to play a featuring part

runners.name: Invincible Destiny
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut in a Cork maiden in which Cosmetic finished fifth; stable outsider with Shane Foley on Altura.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut in a Cork maiden in which Cosmetic finished fifth, stable outsider

runners.name: Werifesteria
runners.spotlight: Ran green first time out at Dundalk in May; Shane Foley prefers stablemate Altura now.
runners.comment: Ran green first time out at Dundalk in May, Shane Foley prefers Altura now

runners.name: Soigne
runners.spotlight: Last of nine in a Naas maiden also contested by stablemate French Blond; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Last of nine in a Naas maiden also contested by stablemate French Blond, can be ruled out

runners.name: Path To Peace
runners.spotlight: Towards the rear in two 1m2f maidens at Leopardstown; will need a handicap mark to have any chance of winning a race.
runners.comment: Towards the rear in two 1m2f maidens at Leopardstown, will need a handicap mark

runners.name: Altura
runners.spotlight: Well-related filly was unfancied on debut in a 1m2f maiden at Leopardstown in April and was well beaten; some allowance should be made for interference suffered around three furlongs down.
runners.comment: Well beaten on Leopardstown debut, some allowance should be made for interference suffered

runners.name: Kimy
runners.spotlight: Has won only one of her 25 races over hurdles, placed six times, currently rated 95; never close enough to challenge in a 1m7f maiden at Leopardstown 13 days ago on her only Flat start.
runners.comment: Has won only one of her 25 races over hurdles, placed six times, no threat on Flat debut

runners.name: Keep It Together
runners.spotlight: Poor form in bumpers/hurdles; last of ten on AW on her only Flat outing.
runners.comment: Poor form in bumpers/hurdles, last of ten on AW on her only Flat outing

races.race_name: Betfred 'Bet Builder' Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Oj Lifestyle
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner who has been placed in half of his six visits here, including when third in this race in 2024; carries a 5lb penalty for winning at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) ten days ago but Toby Moore's 7lb claim will aid his bid to go back-to-back; considered.
runners.comment: Placed in half of six runs here; won at Brighton ten days ago; 5lb penalty but considered

runners.name: Bobacious
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who made all under Mason Paetel in this race (was 2lb higher) last year for Ed Dunlop; very respectable third on stable debut at Lingfield in May and soft ground may have blunted his finishing effort last time; interesting.
runners.comment: Won this under Mason Paetel last year (was 2lb higher); interesting in his repeat bid

runners.name: Haveagobeau
runners.spotlight: Lingfield AW classified winner in March but was safely held in two turf outings (also 1m2f) last month; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Lingfield AW classified winner in March but safely held in two turf outings last month

runners.name: Mrembo
runners.spotlight: Six visits to Epsom have seen her placed four times, including when second in this race in 2024; narrowly got the better of one of just two rivals when winning at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) in May and it was a solid fourth in similar event back there a fortnight ago; will probably give her running but more of an each-way prospect.
runners.comment: Has gone well here and will probably give her running but more of an each-way prospect

runners.name: Hello Cotai
runners.spotlight: Fine record here, with his four runs yielding two wins and a second for Adam West in 2024; followed that trainer to France last year but he was back to being an Epsom resident when close third on stable debut at Lingfield (AW) in April and secured first win for this yard at Windsor (also 1m2f, good to firm) one month ago; 4lb rise but he's won off higher; player.
runners.comment: Fine record here; first win for this yard 30 days ago; 4lb rise but won off higher; player

runners.name: Beta Reader
runners.spotlight: Good AW effort when beaten a head for this yard in February and it was a fair third at Windsor (also 1m2f) last month; that was just his fourth turf start so maybe he can build on it.
runners.comment: Good AW effort when beaten a head for this yard in February; fair turf third last time

runners.name: Imperial Cult
runners.spotlight: Strong in the market when he made a winning stable debut at Lingfield (1m4f) and he followed up off 3lb higher at Kempton (1m3f, also AW) three weeks ago; handicapper reacted by dishing out a career-high mark but this locally trained gelding is a C&D winner who needs considering in current mood.
runners.comment: C&D winner who arrives here on a hat-trick; career-high mark but he's a contender again

runners.name: Super Hit
runners.spotlight: Encouraging stable debut when second of 12 at Lingfield (1m, AW) hasn't been built on, although latest 8l defeat when fourth of 14 at Redcar (7f) came when the first two home raced alone; from the family of an Oaks winner so there's hope on pedigree that this new trip might help.
runners.comment: Latest was probably better than it looks; family of Oaks winner so new trip might help

runners.name: Turpin
runners.spotlight: Patchy form across nine starts although latest fifth of 13 at Brighton (1m2f, good) was one of his better efforts; didn't seem to handle the track on his sole visit here, however.
runners.comment: Latest fifth was one of his better efforts; didn't handle the track on his sole visit here

races.race_name: Betfred Play Fred's £5 Million EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Wise And Wonderful
runners.spotlight: 5-2 and she kept at it when second to a potentially above-average newcomer at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on her second start a fortnight ago; that was a step up on her debut and it's likely she can do better again; considered.
runners.comment: Stepped up on debut when second latest and likely she can do better again; considered

runners.name: Shemiyla Star
runners.spotlight: 14-1 but he went down by only a neck at Pontefract (6f, good) on his debut at the start of the month; winner and third then had a pop at Royal Ascot, admittedly with no impact, but that was a promising start and pedigree suggests this new trip is the way to go.
runners.comment: Promising second at the start of the month; pedigree says yes to this new trip; player

runners.name: Confernation
runners.spotlight: Beaten 2.25l for fourth on both starts, latterly at Leicester (7f, good) 18 days ago; distinct frame possibilities again.
runners.comment: Beaten 2.25l for fourth on both starts; distinct frame possibilities again

runners.name: Bullrider
runners.spotlight: By first-season sire Blackbeard; 40,000gns foal, £50,000 yearling then 30,000euros 2yo breeze-up purchase; second foal; dam unraced sister to winners Homeland (7f 2yo) and Some Romance (French 11.5f), half-sister to Coventry/Golden Jubilee winner Art Connoisseur; yard going nicely with this year's juveniles and the market should provide some good clues.
runners.comment: Price dipped to 30,000euros as a breeze-up but market can offer further clues

runners.name: Whiskey Galore
runners.spotlight: 35,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Goodwood Odyssey (1m-1m4f including 2yo/Czechia; RPR 99), Delta Bay (1m; 84), Coconut Bay (7f/1m2f; 70) and Bay Watching (Greek 6.5f); dam placed 6f/7f (including 2yo; 73), half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner; market check required on debut.
runners.comment: 35,000gns yearling; half-brother to four winners; market check required on debut

runners.name: Mojito Madness
runners.spotlight: Attracted strong initial support before drifting in the market then looking raw and weakening on his debut at Leicester (7f, good) three weeks ago; interesting to see if those early birds come back for a second peck.
runners.comment: Early market support before looking raw and weakening on his debut; market check needed

runners.name: Foscarini
runners.spotlight: 7-1 shot when looking green before fifth of 11 at Salisbury (6f, soft) on his debut three weeks ago; goes up in trip and he's a likely improver.
runners.comment: 7-1 shot when fifth on his debut three weeks ago; up in trip and he's a likely improver

runners.name: Printing Money
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in two starts at 6f on a sound surface but he was punted first time and only 4-1 at Ripon on second start a fortnight ago; something is likely showing up at home that we've yet to see on the racecourse and he can't be ruled out on that score.
runners.comment: Moderate form so far but market has been suggesting better was expected
runners.owner: Hold My Beer, N Bradley 6, L Bearman

races.race_name: Betfred 'Nifty 50' Handicap

runners.name: Independent Expert
runners.spotlight: Exposed mare who was 6-44 on turf in Ireland; beaten in claimers on her two starts last month (7f and 1m, turf/AW) so it will be interesting to see how she goes in the market on this first stab at 6f since her only attempt over three years ago; stable debut.
runners.comment: 6-44 on turf in Ireland; beaten in two claimers last month (7f/1m, turf/AW); stable debut

runners.name: Amazing Journey
runners.spotlight: Novice and nursery-winning juvenile (6f, good to firm) who often didn't have things go his way subsequently until he won going away at Kempton (6f, AW) three weeks ago; up 6lb but he's shaped like a smart horse waiting to happen on occasions and maybe that was the start of it.
runners.comment: Won going away (6f, AW) three weeks ago; up 6lb but he's often promised plenty; player

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: Snapped a losing sequence when down in class to score at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on Saturday; 5lb penalty leaves him with a fair burden to carry but he's in the same grade and should make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Down in class to win (6f, good to firm) on Saturday; 5lb penalty but in the same grade

runners.name: Goldwork
runners.spotlight: Goodwood 2yo novice winner who kept fairly good company last year; made promising reappearance third (AW) in April and was back in that sort of form when third again at Newmarket (again 6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; was first home of the far-side group that day and the field won't be splitting this time.
runners.comment: First in group and near his best when third (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; respected

runners.name: Havana Rum
runners.spotlight: Back to his best last year and his reappearance second of 13 at Pontefract (5f) in April sent his mark to new heights; hasn't matched that in three starts since but last time's fifth of ten from the widest stall at Chester (6f, also good to soft; not clear run) suggests this mark isn't beyond him; in the picture.
runners.comment: Fine reappearance and latest at Chester (wide stall) suggests this mark isn't beyond him

runners.name: Sir Garfield
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner who improved last year and was better than ever when scoring at Catterick (6f, good) on his reappearance nearly three months ago; subsequently absent but unlikely he'd be making the journey down without good reason and may be capable of better yet.
runners.comment: Better than ever when scoring (6f, good) on April return; may be capable of better yet

runners.name: Mumayaz
runners.spotlight: Won for the tenth time when picking up five-runner Leicester handicap (6f, good to firm) in May; subsequent respectable efforts, including when beaten 3l off this mark at Windsor on Monday, will need improving on as he goes back up in grade.
runners.comment: Beaten 3l off this mark on Monday; that will need improving on back up in grade

runners.name: Dream Composer
runners.spotlight: Hit the ground running for this yard, his Ripon win in April backed up by arguably unlucky fourth at Goodwood the following month; not beaten far in the Dash here next time and led on the line at Windsor (again 5f, good to firm) last Monday; 4lb penalty to overcome and his best has come at 5f, but he's still considered.
runners.comment: Led on the line (5f, good to firm) last Monday; 4lb penalty but he's still considered

races.race_name: Betfred 'The Classic Bookmaker' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Double Red
runners.spotlight: Secured a fourth win (three on turf) when taking Lingfield handicap (1m2f, AW) on his April reappearance but that hasn't been backed up in two subsequent starts; latest fourth was a step back in the right direction, however, when shaping like this first attempt at 1m4f is worth a go; 1-1 here and he holds each-way prospects.
runners.comment: Course winner who shaped like this first attempt at 1m4f is worth a go last time; e-w shot

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner (all 1m4f on a sound surface) with three of those in blinkers, latterly at Catterick (good; made all) on his penultimate start; overdid it in the lead before beating one home at Goodwood 12 days ago but could bounce back; cheekpieces (second on all three attempts) are reapplied.
runners.comment: In good form until he overdid it in the lead 12 days ago; could bounce back

runners.name: Arcturus Flame
runners.spotlight: Won Nottingham handicap (8.5f, good; first-time cheekpieces) for Darryll Holland last year; mostly very consistent for this yard and has placed in three of his four starts (9.5f to 1m6f) this term; handicapper appears to be in charge but maybe first-time blinkers and Billy Loughnane can eke out more.
runners.comment: Placed in 3 of his 4 starts (9.5f to 1m6f) this term; first-time blinkers may eke out more

runners.name: Central Command
runners.spotlight: Just one blip in an otherwise productive campaign since reappearing in handicaps this term, his three wins including head defeat of another improving 3yo at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) 11 days ago; that form looks all the better after the runner-up went in at York on Saturday so this 5lb rise might not stop him; big player.
runners.comment: Latest head success (1m4f, good to firm) got a good form boost on Saturday; big player

runners.name: Sir William
runners.spotlight: Very inconsistent in four starts for this yard this term and his good second at Windsor (11.5f, good) in May was flanked by a pair of 1m2f howlers; mark fine if he's on a going day but sole appearance here (well beaten when favourite last April) raises a further doubt.
runners.comment: Very inconsistent in four starts for this yard this term; mark fine if he's on a going day

runners.name: Alma Latina
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; showed plenty in her three qualifying runs (all 1m2f on good ground) and was off the mark in four-runner Pontefract maiden when looking better the further she went four weeks ago; pedigree also offers plenty of hope that this step up in trip will bring her on again and her opening mark looks fair; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Won 1m2f maiden latest; step up in trip can bring her on & opening mark fair; shortlisted

runners.name: Timber Twelve
runners.spotlight: Won his first two starts (1m novices on AW) but he's yet to kick on in turf handicaps, initially when fourth of six here (8.5f) in April then fifth of ten at Chester (10.5f) the following month; one of his siblings was a dual 1m4f winner for this yard so maybe the new trip will help.
runners.comment: Yet to kick on in turf handicaps but pedigree suggests this new trip might be a help

runners.name: Electrical
runners.spotlight: Caused a 25-1 upset on his debut at Newbury (6f) around a year ago but that remains the highlight and he could only beat one home in both handicaps this term; unraced beyond 1m but does have stayers in the family and his sister won at up to 1m6f.
runners.comment: Unraced beyond 1m and comes here in moderate form but there are stayers in the family

runners.name: Tabasko
runners.spotlight: Yet to win but she's got ability and it wasn't a bad effort when fifth of 12 at Salisbury (1m2f, good) on her return in May; dam is related to St Leger winner Hurricane Lane and Group-class stayer Sweet William so there's plenty of hope this new trip will suit; not discounted on that score.
runners.comment: Yet to win but she's got ability; new trip promises to suit on breeding

runners.name: Mr Freedom
runners.spotlight: Multiple Flat/hurdles winner who came back from an absence (after a nasty spill at Plumpton) with encouraging third at Salisbury (good; not clear run) in May; didn't build on that at Goodwood (also 1m6f) this month but he travelled well until soft ground likely got the better of him; drops in trip and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Went well until soft ground likely won the battle latest; drops in trip and not ruled out

races.race_name: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Sarangpur
runners.spotlight: Tackling 1m4f and quick ground for the first time when he bolted up at Brighton on his British/stable debut ten days ago; 5lb extra wouldn't have come close to stopping him that day and he's the standout under a penalty.
runners.comment: Bolted up on British/stable debut ten days ago and 5lb penalty is unlikely to stop him

runners.name: Stintino Sunset
runners.spotlight: Last won back in September 2024 but plenty of creditable efforts since include a third in this race (good to firm) last year; tried this headgear combination when beaten less than 2l at Windsor one month ago and she's got solid each-way prospects again.
runners.comment: Third in this last year and beaten less than 2l latest; solid each-way prospects again

runners.name: Panthere Noir
runners.spotlight: 0-14 but it was a solid third when 50-1 at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) on his second go for this yard last month; took a massive step backwards when beaten 35l on AW a fortnight ago, however, and he'll need to have his Windsor hat on tonight.
runners.comment: Solid third on penultimate start but big step backwards on AW a fortnight ago

runners.name: Dotties Star
runners.spotlight: Got up on the line to bag 1m4f AW classified event in March but he's made little impact in two subsequent handicaps and is 0-7 here.
runners.comment: Just won 1m4f AW classified in March but little impact in subsequent handicaps; 0-7 here

runners.name: Pablo Prince
runners.spotlight: AW regular (four wins at around this trip) who was well held in this race last year; not disgraced back on turf when fourth in 1m2f classified event a fortnight ago but that brought his record to 0-8 on grass.
runners.comment: AW regular (four wins at around this trip); well held in this race last year; 0-8 on grass

runners.name: Lilykoy
runners.spotlight: Showed next to nothing at 7f and 6f in three qualifying runs last year but her reappearance was much more like it when stepped up sharply in trip and fifth of 13 in Windsor classified (11.5f, good to soft; stayed on in first-time cheekpieces) three weeks ago; still early days ahead of this first handicap.
runners.comment: Improved for step up in trip and headgear in classified on return; makes handicap debut

runners.name: Head Girl
runners.spotlight: Improved effort in first-time cheekpieces (retained) when third of ten at Lingfield (11.5f, good to firm) one month ago; that was just her fifth start and she's one of few legitimate threats to Sarangpur.
runners.comment: Improved third in first-time cheekpieces one month ago; that was just her fifth start

runners.name: Dissident
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner but he's 0-10 on the Flat and keeps weakening no matter what trip he faces; slipping down the weights but he's difficult to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-10 on the Flat and he keeps weakening no matter what trip he faces; difficult to fancy

runners.name: Fram Castle
runners.spotlight: 2m AW winner but his form gradually tailed off for previous stable; new yard could hardly be in better form but he'd still become of interest only with market support ahead of this reappearance.
runners.comment: Joins a yard that could hardly be in better form; watch for market support on this return

runners.name: Thursday
runners.spotlight: Dual winner (11.5f and 1m2f) who was placed in a couple of 1m2f handicaps here in 2024; the wheels have come off subsequently, however, and three starts this year promised little.
runners.comment: The wheels appear to have come off and three starts this year promised little

races.race_name: Betfred 'Follow Us On X' Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Just Adair
runners.spotlight: Dual 11.5f Windsor winner last summer and it was a good comeback when second under Billy Loughnane over that C&D one month ago; unraced at two and he's likely got further progress in him this term; leading contender.
runners.comment: Dual 11.5f winner last summer; good comeback second; leading contender

runners.name: Fighting Poet
runners.spotlight: Two runs here came in this race but he couldn't better his 2024 runner-up spot when only sixth last year; reappearance effort wasn't bad eight weeks ago but he's been poor in both starts this month, including when well behind Gearing's Point at Brighton last Monday.
runners.comment: Poor in both starts this month; well behind Gearing's Point at Brighton last Monday

runners.name: Naranka
runners.spotlight: Registered an improved effort when chasing home a subsequent winner at Lingfield (11.5f, good to firm; Fletcher seventh) one month ago; could be getting his act together and he needs consideration.
runners.comment: Improved last time and could be getting his act together now; needs consideration

runners.name: Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit more when dropped into a classified and sixth at Windsor (good to soft) three weeks ago; still fairly early days.
runners.comment: Showed a bit more when dropped into a classified three weeks ago; still fairly early days

runners.name: High Favour
runners.spotlight: This year has been a struggle and his return from a break was a bit of a disaster when 80-1 and tailed off at Yarmouth (soft ground) three weeks ago; has a few questions to answer.
runners.comment: Mostly struggled last term and his comeback was a bit of a disaster when tailed off

runners.name: Ricardo Phillips
runners.spotlight: Bar one blip he was consistent last term and he clung on to score at Leicester (1m4f, good) last July; nothing wrong with this mark and he ran with credit on last season's reappearance; solid each-way claims.
runners.comment: Won (1m4f) last July; mark fine & ran with credit on last term's return; solid e-w claims

runners.name: Gearing's Point
runners.spotlight: Went close at Windsor (11.5f) in May and she was winning her fifth race when landing a 1m4f Brighton classified (also good to firm) going away last Monday; 4lb penalty makes things trickier back in a handicap but she's not discounted.
runners.comment: Won her fifth race when landing 1m4f classified last Monday; 4lb penalty but not ruled out

runners.name: Nymphaea
runners.spotlight: She had signalled a breakthrough might be coming and it duly did in four-runner event at Nottingham (1m6f, good to firm) on Thursday; can probably cope with this drop back in trip but a 5lb penalty for that nose success does make life harder; still respected.
runners.comment: First win (1m6f) on Thursday; 5lb penalty makes life harder but she's still respected

runners.name: Arenas Del Tiempo
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins but the latest of them, which came here over 1m2f, was nearly five years ago; back down to her last winning mark (AW in August) but she finished fifth in this last year and minor honours may be the best she can hope for.
runners.comment: Three turf wins but the latest of them, here over 1m2f, was nearly five years ago
runners.owner: D Edmonston, T Helliwell & S J Holden

races.race_name: Bath And Wiltshire Air Ambulance Handicap

runners.name: Rogue Rebellion
runners.spotlight: Three runs for each of his former yards and managed a win on Lingfield's Polytrack in January (5f); went close for current connections over C&D in May but two subsequent efforts are hard to gloss over.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in May but two subsequent efforts are hard to gloss over

runners.name: Rosieisme Darling
runners.spotlight: Two 5f Polytrack wins last winter and confirmed she's no one-trick pony with some good turf runs of late; involved.
runners.comment: Confirmed she's no one-trick pony with some good turf runs of late; involved

runners.name: Reality Queen
runners.spotlight: C&D winner for Jonathan Portman last September; sent off 10-1 for stable debut at Newmarket 11 days ago and wasn't disgraced despite shaping as if needing the run; dropped 2lb; better expected here.
runners.comment: C&D winner for Jonathan Portman last September; can leave stable debut behind

runners.name: Lavender Bloom
runners.spotlight: This 35,000gns breeze-up buy, who didn't make the track at two, showed a bit in the face of adversity at Leicester two starts ago; didn't run so well over C&D latest but a switch to low-grade handicaps should see her in a much better light this evening; David Egan has ridden two winners, one second and two thirds from ten rides for the yard.
runners.comment: This 35,000gns breeze-up buy should be much more competitive in this 0-60 handicap

runners.name: Geemann
runners.spotlight: Standout performance when beaten less than 2l into sixth in final qualifying run over C&D; down the field in two track and trip handicaps since (gelded/break in between) and can't be supported with confidence.
runners.comment: Down the field in two track and trip handicaps and can't be supported with confidence

races.race_name: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Theheatison
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut at Lingfield in May (7f, turf) but was bang there over a furlong out before weakening into fifth of 11 at Salisbury 17 days ago (6f); that could be a nice race with the third winning since.
runners.comment: Fifth in race that is likely to work out well (third has already won since) second start

runners.name: Angel Ang
runners.spotlight: Seemingly no fluke about ready debut win at Wolverhampton 15 days ago (6f), despite going off at 33-1; trainer has had four first-time-out 2yo winners this year and the other three have all failed to win again since; 7lb penalty might be tough to defy.
runners.comment: 7lb penalty might be tough to defy given profile of similar types from this yard in 2026

runners.name: Harry's Pop
runners.spotlight: Small step forward at Chester last month but the betting suggested a bigger one would be forthcoming; likely needs extra; one of two twice-raced Dylan Cunha runners.
runners.comment: Small step forward last month but the betting suggested a bigger one would be forthcoming
runners.owner: K Shenton, N Bradley 6 & D Cunha

runners.name: Soldier's Echo
runners.spotlight: £45,000 yearling; ninth foal; sister to Group-placed 6f-1m2f winner Soldier's Gold (including 2yo; RPR 104), half-sister to three winners including Mr McCann (6f-8.5f, including 2yo; 105); dam 1m 2yo winner (105); trainer having a fine year with his 2yos, including a second in the Coventry; one to consider; like Chasingpursuit, she's in the sales next week.
runners.comment: Trainer having a fine year with his 2yos, including a second in the Coventry

runners.name: Empire Rising
runners.spotlight: Down the field on Windsor debut then managed fourth of 11 at Salisbury; that much improved effort came on soft ground, as opposed to good to firm, but he's probably worth another chance on a sound surface.
runners.comment: Down the field on Windsor debut then managed fourth of 11 at Salisbury

runners.name: Dissenter
runners.spotlight: 14,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 8.6f AW winner (RPR 72), sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Rebel Assault; trainer has run two juveniles this year and they were both well beaten; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Trainer has run two juveniles this year and they were both well beaten

runners.name: Chasingpursuit
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced sister to 7f Listed winner Jash; good yard 0-4 with 2yos this year but market check advised; wears blinkers; is in the sales next week.
runners.comment: Good yard 0-4 with 2yos this year but market check advised; wears blinkers
runners.owner: A Bengough, P Inglett And Partners

runners.name: Pj's Corner
runners.spotlight: Left debut effort behind at Ripon 13 days ago but will be of greater interest once given a mark for nurseries, which will probably be after this run.
runners.comment: Of greater interest once given a mark for nurseries, which will probably be after this run

runners.name: Parkers Light
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 6f/7f AW winner (RPR 95), half-sister to couple of nice 2yos; already gelded; in-form trainer had a first-time-out 2yo winner in May which was well supported; drawn widest but watch betting.
runners.comment: In-form trainer had a first-time-out 2yo winner in May which was well supported

runners.name: King Koopa
runners.spotlight: Sent off 28-1 but knew his job on debut at Wolverhampton 15 days ago (6f) as he managed to hold his position from the rail draw and make the running before Angel Ang and two others went by in the straight; he carried his head a bit awkwardly there but can put that down to greenness for now.
runners.comment: Positives and negatives from debut fourth to Angel Ang

runners.name: Texas Crown
runners.spotlight: 80-1 when failing to beat a rival on debut at Salisbury last month (6f; Theheatison fared much better); can only watch.
runners.comment: 80-1 when failing to beat a rival on debut at Salisbury last month

runners.name: Velma Dinkley
runners.spotlight: 40-1 last of 12 on debut at Windsor in May (6f; Class 2); can't consider.
runners.comment: 40-1 last of 12 on debut at Windsor in May (6f; Class 2); can't consider

races.race_name: Hot Air Balloon Company Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Hidden Verse
runners.spotlight: First visit here was a winning one (this trip; did it nicely; May 2025) and he hasn't run a bad race at the track since, most recent example when not getting the rub of the green in a 0-70 two starts ago (finished fifth); soft ground a valid excuse latest; of interest in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Didn't get the rub of the green in a C&D 0-70 two starts ago; ground a valid excuse latest

runners.name: Fancy Dancer
runners.spotlight: Triple winner (one C&D) for Ollie Sangster last year; uncompetitive for new connections, including in first-time tongue-tie (retained) at Nottingham four weeks ago; better options elsewhere.
runners.comment: Uncompetitive for new connections, including in first-time tongue-tie (retained) latest

runners.name: Southbank
runners.spotlight: Authoritative win over C&D on first start for Chris Gordon last May and plenty went wrong when bidding to win that race again two starts ago (surely would have been heavily involved with a clear run); beaten into second at even-money on AW next time but the winner rather stole it; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Due a change of luck and no surprise if backers return

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: Best of the rest behind Neptune Legend over C&D in May (sole course run) but she's struggled since.
runners.comment: Best of the rest behind Neptune Legend over C&D in May (sole course run); struggled since

runners.name: Sisters In The Sky
runners.spotlight: Landed this race off 7lb higher last year; couldn't get involved over the bare 5f here 18 days ago but his two previous runs over this slightly longer trip were encouraging; likely there or thereabouts.
runners.comment: Repeat win not out of the question back over this slightly longer trip

runners.name: Symbol Of Hope
runners.spotlight: Won seven times here; back with Grace Harris last two starts; lasted pretty long in front over C&D but was then beaten a fair way out when ridden the same way over the bare 5f here 18 days ago (just held off reopposing stablemate Sisters In The Sky for eighth).
runners.comment: Could bag an early lead but not sure he'll stay there

runners.name: Neptune Legend
runners.spotlight: Returned to Tony Carroll and left a couple of quiet AW runs behind when scoring over C&D (beat She Went Whoosh by nearly 3l) and at Brighton (6f) in May; came up short in hat-trick bid at Windsor on Monday but was out of his comfort zone early over the bare 5f before plugging on into fifth; not dismissed if turned out quickly.
runners.comment: Shaped as if still in form at Windsor on Monday; not dismissed if turned out quickly

runners.name: Ten Club
runners.spotlight: Has won on turf but is happiest on AW; expected to be caught out by this shorter trip on his return from nearly five months off.
runners.comment: Expected to be caught out by this shorter trip on his return from nearly five months off

races.race_name: Lansdown Golf Club Classified Stakes

runners.name: Scenario
runners.spotlight: April's C&D handicap win reads well; not in the same form since but returning to classified company, where she's 1-2, should help.
runners.comment: April's C&D handicap win reads well; do better than of late in this classified

runners.name: Letsbeatsepsis
runners.spotlight: Bitterly disappointing given the promise he showed on debut at two but could pick up a small race; pulled his chance away latest and dropping back in trip looks a wise move.
runners.comment: Pulled his chance away latest and dropping back in trip looks a wise move

runners.name: Monks Mead
runners.spotlight: Form figures in classified company read 35124323 but he might be tapped for toe back at 1m this evening.
runners.comment: Form figures in classified company read 35124323 but he might be tapped for toe back at 1m

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: 0-19 but found just the even-money favourite who has won again since too strong in first-time blinkers (retained) at Ffos Las last week; should cope with dropping back to 1m from 1m2f and is drawn to attack.
runners.comment: Strong form claims and should cope with dropping back to 1m from 1m2f

runners.name: Baynoona
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over C&D under Millie Wonnacott last September; had the odd ground/trip excuse since and was messed around in the closing stages at Chepstow last time; chance.
runners.comment: Sole win came over C&D under Millie Wonnacott; reasons to believe she can bounce back
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Star Of Atlantis
runners.spotlight: Best RPR for Adrian Wintle when runner-up in C&D handicap last July; more miss than hit on AW of late; chance if David Egan, who gets aboard for the first time, can settle her.
runners.comment: Best RPR for Adrian Wintle when runner-up in C&D handicap last July; chance if settling

runners.name: Blue Hero
runners.spotlight: All nine wins achieved here but seemingly wants further than 1m these days; went backwards from a pleasing return last time; goes without headgear for the first time in over five years.
runners.comment: Trip and lack of headgear are obvious concerns

runners.name: Addmann
runners.spotlight: Peak RPR of 31 in six starts from 5.7f-1m; can't consider; wears first-time headgear (cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Peak RPR of 31 in six starts from 5.7f-1m; can't consider; wears first-time headgear

runners.name: Due Destiny
runners.spotlight: Latest effort, which was her second for this yard/off a break, wasn't bad and gave hope for this new trip of 1m; there are worse options.
runners.comment: Latest effort wasn't bad and gave hope for this new trip of 1m; there are worse options

runners.name: Hammal
runners.spotlight: Last or second last all four starts for this yard following layoff; sneaks into this lowly 0-50 classified contest but is hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Sneaks into this lowly 0-50 classified contest but is hard to fancy

runners.name: Olivia Jane
runners.spotlight: Disappointing filly but stable debut (left Oliver Signy) off 305-day break at Chepstow last month (1m2f) wasn't devoid of hope, particularly as she made a big move into contention before those exertions told (finished a place behind Knightmare); blinkers introduced there retained.
runners.comment: Disappointing filly but stable debut off 305-day break latest wasn't devoid of hope

runners.name: Eye Of The Water
runners.spotlight: This veteran hasn't tasted success for nearly three years; he ran okay over C&D two starts ago and when sixth at Ffos Las last week but more is needed.
runners.comment: Ran okay over C&D two starts ago and when sixth at Ffos Las last week but more is needed

runners.name: Havana's Dream
runners.spotlight: Yet to make the frame in 13 attempts and was again beaten a long way at Chepstow on Saturday.
runners.comment: Yet to make the frame in 13 attempts and was again beaten a long way on Saturday

races.race_name: MJ Church Handicap

runners.name: Swiped
runners.spotlight: Blinkers fitted for both starts in 3yo only handicaps; went down narrowly at Salisbury (7f) then powered home to score readily over C&D; highly likely has more to offer and can defy a 7lb rise taking on mostly older horses.
runners.comment: Highly likely has more to offer and can win again over C&D

runners.name: Thiscouldbefun
runners.spotlight: Scored readily off this mark/under Ashley Lewis last October (1m, AW); turf win came over C&D but looks best watched here having ran so poorly in refitted cheekpieces at Salisbury 17 days ago (finished three places behind Sant Alessio).
runners.comment: Looks best watched having ran so poorly in refitted cheekpieces at Salisbury 17 days ago
runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

runners.name: Post Rider
runners.spotlight: Won the last time she contested a Class 5 (made all over this trip at Newmarket last October); first taste of slow ground when showing good bit more than on Lingfield reappearance at Goodwood 19 days ago; claims if taking another step forward for all she faces competition for the lead from Sant Alessio.
runners.comment: Claims if taking another step forward for all she faces competition for the lead

runners.name: Sant Alessio
runners.spotlight: Comfortably made all over C&D and made most at Wolverhampton (8.6f; also last year); two weak finishes in 2026 and fellow front-runner Post Rider could spoil his chance here.
runners.comment: Two weak finishes in 2026 and fellow front-runner Post Rider could spoil his chance here

runners.name: Ebn Sabt
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two starts for this yard; can't consider.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in two starts for this yard; can't consider

runners.name: Naval Ensign
runners.spotlight: Bought cheaply by current connections (was trained by Jack Jones) and made the frame when starting out for them over C&D 18 days ago (off a break; wore hood and tongue-tie together for the first time; headgear retained here); this is tougher.
runners.comment: Fair start for new connections over C&D 18 days ago but this is tougher

runners.name: Nakaaha
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins over this trip last summer; mostly ran with credit of late, including when lacking a change of gear after travelling well over 7f at Newbury eight days ago; runner-up sole visit here.
runners.comment: Moving back up to 1m should suit and finished second sole visit here

races.race_name: Magnum Scaffolding Contracts Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Sea Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, the latest on AW (1m4f) in February; quiet on her last three starts but she has slipped down the weights as a result.
runners.comment: 1lb lower than for 1m4f AW win in February; last three runs have been short of her best

runners.name: Offiah's Boy
runners.spotlight: Won four times on fast ground last summer, two of them over C&D; possible to make excuses for most of his runs since (the latest on ground slower than ideal and after 164 days off) and this race, under optimal conditions, should be a better guide as to just where he stands at present.
runners.comment: Conditions ideal; needs to leave recent return behind him but that's likely

runners.name: We Ride At Dawn
runners.spotlight: No threat on stable/handicap debut (1m, AW) in May but much more like it when second of ten in a 1m2f handicap at Windsor (good to firm) last month; should stay this extra distance and she's a strong contender despite her 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Improved for step up to 1m2f when second at Windsor last month; more to come over further

runners.name: Barak Warrior
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in three 1m2f events this spring (good to soft to good to firm); gelded last month; should stay this far and he is open to some improvement now handicapping at a modest level.
runners.comment: Big prices and well beaten in three 1m2f starts this spring; gelded; open to improvement
runners.owner: W Sweatman, J Summers, R Foden

runners.name: Lakota Chief
runners.spotlight: Didn't live up to market billing in three AW runs (7f-1m) early in the year; gelded since last seen; not bred to need this far but his sire's few middle-distance winners have been out of mares by stamina influences, as is he; interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market billing over 7f/1m this winter; gelded; new trip now handicapping

runners.name: Pivotal Days
runners.spotlight: 7f win for Ed Walker as a 2yo; modest form on Flat and over hurdles for new yard this year; Royal Ascot was fanciful latest and although this is more realistic, others still bring more compelling claims.
runners.comment: This more suitable than Royal Ascot last time but still not an obvious winner

runners.name: Campeona
runners.spotlight: On a stiff mark judged on her achievements in maiden/novice events at up to 8.6f this winter but she is bred to leave those efforts well behind now handicapping over middle distances (dam a Group 3 winner); obvious chance.
runners.comment: Bred to be much better than this and should leave her 2yo efforts well behind this year

races.race_name: Racing Goes On Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Perfecto Moments
runners.spotlight: No wins in 19 but arrives in form despite having had to contend with some wide draws; good chance this time from stall 1.
runners.comment: No wins in 19 but arrives in form despite having had to contend with some dodgy draws

runners.name: Frantanck
runners.spotlight: Way better than this grade in his prime and he was running quite well (7f/1m) until his last race when poorly drawn; better boxed this time and Nichola Yuen keeps the faith; fourth in a recent trial.
runners.comment: He was running quite well (7f/1m) until his last race when poorly drawn

runners.name: Gold Tack
runners.spotlight: Made it 1-39 when beaten 4l last time at Happy Valley; the return of blinkers could be a good move, though.
runners.comment: Made it 1-39 when beaten 4l last time at Happy Valley; blinkers return
runners.owner: Robert Chung Wai Shu & Jeanette Chung Siu Wah

runners.name: Draco
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-21 but has competitive form and didn't get a clear shot at it when a close fourth here last time over 7f.
runners.comment: Reserve; didn't get a clear shot at it when a close fourth here last time over 7f

runners.name: Zetta Force
runners.spotlight: His two efforts earlier in the year off higher marks than today make him a player in this company but he's finished down the field in his last two races, the latest only a week ago.
runners.comment: He's finished down the field in his last two races, the latest only a week ago

runners.name: Happydearhappydeer
runners.spotlight: Very well handicapped on last season's placed form and he has a kinder draw than last time; just a concern that he's perhaps better on dirt.
runners.comment: Very well handicapped on last season's placed form but might be happier on dirt
runners.owner: Hong Yiu, Lynn Fu Lam Wu Et Al

runners.name: Oriental Surprise
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden from 5f to 1m; however, placed in two of his last three races and Purton was aboard when runner-up to a comfortable winner over this far at Happy Valley latest.
runners.comment: Purton aboard when runner-up to a comfortable winner over this far at Happy Valley latest

runners.name: Harry's Hero
runners.spotlight: Won twice in Britain but has a poor strike-rate in Hong Kong and he was behind two of these last time.
runners.comment: Has a poor strike-rate in Hong Kong and he was behind two of these last time

runners.name: General Smart
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose over 7f here in March and another good run when fourth two starts back (1m); however, he's not that consistent, ran moderately in a recent trial and is drawn in 14.
runners.comment: He's not that consistent, ran moderately in a recent trial and drawn in 14
runners.owner: Alex Chau Man Lok, Jack Chan Hoi Et Al

runners.name: Lucky Blessing
runners.spotlight: Made all over the 1m here in November but hasn't really approached that form since; others preferred from his wide stall.
runners.comment: Made all over the 1m here in November but hasn't really approached that form since

runners.name: Superb Guy
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden who hasn't been far away in his last three races over 7f and his career-best RPR came at a mile; one to consider.
runners.comment: Not far away in his last three races over 7f and his career-best RPR came at a mile

runners.name: Casa Legend
runners.spotlight: Both wins over C&D but his recent efforts have been none too inspiring.
runners.comment: Both wins over C&D but his recent efforts have been none too inspiring

runners.name: Red Brick Warrior
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all eight starts; has been shaping as though further than 1m is worth a go (recent non-runner over 1m3f) but he's down in grade off a reduced mark and could have some role to play.
runners.comment: He's down in grade off a reduced mark and could have some role to play

runners.name: Gale Saga
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come here over 7f, the latest in early 2025; has run some respectable races during his losing sequence and won a recent trial.
runners.comment: Respectable efforts during his losing sequence and won a recent trial

runners.name: Kasa Papa
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has been extremely hard to win with in Hong Kong and others are in better form.
runners.comment: Reserve; has been extremely hard to win with in Hong Kong and others are in better form

runners.name: Ping Hai Comet
runners.spotlight: Has dropped to a good mark for a reason and needs to turn a corner; opposable, for all that he's downgraded into a Class 5.
runners.comment: Has dropped to a good mark for a reason and needs to turn a corner

races.race_name: World-Class Leadership Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Speedy Trident
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but off a much reduced mark he finished well for a close fifth over C&D last time; has a kinder draw now; considered.
runners.comment: 0-18 but off a much reduced mark he finished well for a close fifth over C&D last time

runners.name: Mega Force
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner for Aidan O'Brien but very opposable on his Hong Kong form.
runners.comment: Maiden winner for Aidan O'Brien but very opposable on his Hong Kong form

runners.name: Prince Alex
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been in the best of form, he's drawn wide and probably needs further; others preferred.
runners.comment: Hasn't been in the best of form, he's drawn wide and probably needs further

runners.name: Fortune Kingo
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 11 attempts and mostly disappointing this year; has lots to prove.
runners.comment: Still a maiden after 11 attempts and mostly disappointing this year; has lots to prove

runners.name: Tai Victory
runners.spotlight: Struggled on recent stable debut at Happy Valley and is now 0-13 in Hong Kong; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Struggled on recent stable debut at Happy Valley and is now 0-13 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Mazing Grace
runners.spotlight: No wins in 11 but was a respectable fifth here two runs ago and a slow start did for him when only 5-1 for a C&D handicap last time.
runners.comment: A slow start did for him when only 5-1 for a C&D handicap last time

runners.name: Manythanks Forever
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come over C&D and he hasn't performed too badly of late at Happy Valley when faced with tricky draws; he's landed another one here, though Nichola Yuen's claim is a bonus.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; hasn't been running too badly but again he's drawn wide
runners.owner: Chan Wing Kwong, Patrick Ng Pak Hong Et Al

runners.name: Jolly Jumper
runners.spotlight: Off a career-low mark when defying a wide draw over C&D in May but couldn't defy another off this 6lb higher mark a month ago (1m); beaten but ran well in his latest trial.
runners.comment: Went up 6lb for his C&D win and struggled next time from another wide draw
runners.owner: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate (T1)

runners.name: Fight Time
runners.spotlight: Just 1-29 and struggles have continued in Class 5 races on last three starts; not easily fancied from stall 13.
runners.comment: Just 1-29 and struggles have continued in Class 5 races on last three starts
runners.owner: Daren Wong Wai Chow, Lau Kay Jing & Eric Liu Kar Hang

runners.name: Brave Win
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all ten starts and fared no better dropped into a Class 5 a month ago, albeit from a poor draw; has done most of his racing at sprint trips and has yet to shine over 7f.
runners.comment: Maiden; has done most of his racing at sprint trips and has yet to shine over 7f
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Matthew Lam Kin Hong

runners.name: Top To Sky
runners.spotlight: Hadn't even placed before springing a surprise over C&D last month; held a good position throughout that day and he's gone up 5lb; could be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Narrow winner under a good ride here a month ago; possibly vulnerable up 5lb

runners.name: Wave Garden
runners.spotlight: Won in New Zealand but has a regressive profile in Hong Kong and that continued dropped into a Class 5 last time; new trainer.
runners.comment: Won in New Zealand but has a regressive profile in Hong Kong

runners.name: He Was Me
runners.spotlight: Went up 11lb for winning two races in three starts; had a tough draw the last time but he might be on a tough mark now and was well beaten in a recent trial.
runners.comment: Went up 11lb for winning two races in three starts; had a tough draw the last time

runners.name: Double Show
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D in March and 1m doesn't appear to be his trip, so forgiven last run; however, subsequent trial efforts nothing to shout about.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D in March and 1m doesn't appear to be his trip, so forgiven last run

runners.name: Robot Knight
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won narrowly on the dirt here in May but weakened out of it last time, again over 6f; this is a belated first try at 7f and doesn't appear to be crying out for it.
runners.comment: Reserve; belated first try at 7f and doesn't appear to be crying out for it

races.race_name: The Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Call Me Success
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; had shaped like a winner waiting to happen but has disappointed in his last two races and now goes further than 6f for the first time.
runners.comment: Had shaped like a winner waiting to happen but has disappointed in his last two races

runners.name: Ariel
runners.spotlight: Can front run but doesn't have to; won't be inconvenienced by returning to turf but stall 11 is not ideal and he struggles for consistency.
runners.comment: Stall 11 is not ideal and he struggles for consistency

runners.name: Flying Sniper
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been all that far away in his 6f handicaps and last two races haven't gone entirely to plan, finishing well in the latest; Purton is back on board and 7f is well worth trying.
runners.comment: Not far away in his 6f handicaps and last two races haven't gone entirely to plan
runners.owner: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan

runners.name: Winning Machine
runners.spotlight: All six runs for this yard highly respectable, and he clinged on over C&D ten days ago; essentially just 2lb higher if taking into account Ellis Wong's claim; clear chance.
runners.comment: All six runs for this yard highly respectable, and he clinged on over C&D ten days ago
runners.owner: Sammy Ma Hon Kit/Chan Sin Shui/Exec Of Est Of J C T Cheung

runners.name: Fortune Star
runners.spotlight: Reserve; consistent in the main but at 1m, and 7f caught him out last time; others might be too sharp at this trip.
runners.comment: Reserve; consistent in the main but at 1m, and 7f caught him out last time
runners.owner: Kelvin Chan Kam Kei & Eddie Chan Kam Kong

runners.name: Luck Is Back
runners.spotlight: 4-4 in Brazil but 0-19 in Hong Kong despite a sliding mark; probably needs further.
runners.comment: 4-4 in Brazil but 0-19 in Hong Kong despite a sliding mark; probably needs further

runners.name: Good Luck Babe
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been here, at 7f and 1m; had been struggling for form but ran well in a recent trial and Nichola Yuen claims 10lb.
runners.comment: Both wins have been here, at 7f and 1m; some revival when second in a recent trial

runners.name: King Of Fighters
runners.spotlight: No wins in 19 but has competitive form and was closing at the finish when drawn wide in his first Class 4 last time; respected.
runners.comment: Closing at the finish when drawn wide in his first Class 4 last time; respected

runners.name: Trendy Rush
runners.spotlight: All four runs at 6f and no better than seventh in any of them; might still be on a tough mark.
runners.comment: All four runs at 6f and no better than seventh in any of them

runners.name: Gallant Epoch
runners.spotlight: He was in good form but his mark suffered and he now has to bounce back from two lesser efforts.
runners.comment: Was in good form but his mark suffered and now has to bounce back from two lesser efforts
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Simon Suen Siu Man And Chloe Suen

runners.name: Apex Glory
runners.spotlight: Has won a trial but down the field in both his official races and he's poorly drawn.
runners.comment: Has won a trial but down the field in both his official races and he's poorly drawn

runners.name: Circuit Century
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in his four races (6f-7f) and it's been a mixed bag in trials (won once) since his last official race.
runners.comment: Well beaten in his four races (6f-7f) and it's been a mixed bag in trials (won once)
runners.owner: Ho Yin Sang & Tsang Kin Wa

runners.name: M M Concord
runners.spotlight: Reserve; low mileage and has hinted at winning potential at a higher level; chance if getting a run.
runners.comment: Reserve; low mileage and has hinted at winning potential at a higher level

runners.name: Brilliant Winner
runners.spotlight: Has finished second in a trial since two very underwhelming efforts on the track; probably best to turn elsewhere.
runners.comment: Has finished second in a trial since two very underwhelming efforts on the track
runners.owner: Chow Kay Yui, Kong Kin Kuen & Lau Wai Shing

runners.name: Joy Together
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been showing enough in his trials to believe he can make a successful official debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't been showing enough in his trials to believe he can make a winning official debut

runners.name: Rising From East
runners.spotlight: Finished last on official debut over 6f here in March (118-1); has since won a trial but others preferred.
runners.comment: Finished last on official debut over 6f here in March (118-1); has since won a trial

races.race_name: Uncompromising Integrity 1000m Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Zouper Fellow
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made virtually all over C&D in April but nowhere near that level since.
runners.comment: Reserve; made virtually all over C&D in April but nowhere near that level since
runners.owner: Dr Philip Ho Kin Hoi, Jason Ho Chung Man Et Al

runners.name: Better And Better
runners.spotlight: Often strong in the betting and usually involved in the finish, as his form figures would imply; however, all 15 races have been at 6f and 7f so it's whether he's quick enough for 5f.
runners.comment: Consistent gelding but at 6f and 7f, so it's whether he's quick enough for 5f

runners.name: Supreme Voyager
runners.spotlight: Clear claims on his head defeat over 6f here in March but has lost his way since; hasn't raced over 5f since debut.
runners.comment: Clear claims on his head defeat over 6f here in March but has lost his way since
runners.owner: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Lahore
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when an impressive winner over C&D in May and Zac Purton keeps the ride; should figure, for all that he's not that well drawn and has gone up 8lb.
runners.comment: C&D winner in May; not so well drawn now and he's up 8lb, but still respected

runners.name: Beauty Thunder
runners.spotlight: Only Hong Kong win came over 5f at Happy Valley where he has run many of his better races; remains in good form and would have claims if he can transfer that to this venue.
runners.comment: Only Hong Kong win came over 5f at Happy Valley where he has run many of his better races

runners.name: Juicy Dragon
runners.spotlight: Seemingly confirmed that he's happier on dirt than turf when again successful over 6f here in early May, and has run well over the same C&D since; bit to prove back on the grass (0-5) and dropped to 5f.
runners.comment: Vey able on the dirt but bit to prove back on the grass (0-5) and dropped to 5f

runners.name: Double Alpha
runners.spotlight: Won a trial before finishing runner-up from stall 14 here a month ago; the winner of that race is unexposed and this 3yo again has a useful draw to work from.
runners.comment: Won a trial before finishing runner-up from stall 14 here a month ago

runners.name: Flowing Riches
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last September and some good runs since on the dirt; below par in his last two races, though.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has since run well on dirt; below par in his last two races, though

runners.name: Ka Ying Glory
runners.spotlight: Reserve; was threatening to go the wrong way but encouragingly he won his latest trial; might be on a fair mark.
runners.comment: Reserve; was threatening to go the wrong way but encouragingly he won his latest trial

runners.name: Good Power
runners.spotlight: No better than fifth in his recent trials and can only be watched on debut; tricky draw.
runners.comment: No better than fifth in his recent trials and can only be watched on debut; tricky draw
runners.owner: Cheung Tin Chai, Cheung Shu Yan & Cheung Shu Tak

runners.name: Parrot Blessing
runners.spotlight: Finished last on official debut over C&D three months ago; unable to win any of his subsequent trials.
runners.comment: Finished last over C&D three months ago; unable to win any of his subsequent trials

runners.name: Dragon Sky Force
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 63244 in this year's trials and likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 63244 in this year's trials and likely best watched on debut

runners.name: King Zoom
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a trial but couldn't win one and had plenty of chances; best watched.
runners.comment: Runner-up in one trial but couldn't win one and had plenty of chances; best watched
runners.owner: Bernie Yim Mei Fung & Yim Man Hay

runners.name: Synergy In Action
runners.spotlight: Worrying that he took a few goes to win a trial which he did only recently; unlikely to be the answer.
runners.comment: Worrying that he took a few goes to win a trial which he did only recently
runners.owner: 25/26 Pierre Ng Pang Chi Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Golden Ace
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in his latest barrier trial so has ability and a favourable draw for today's debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up in his latest barrier trial so has ability and a favourable draw for this debut

runners.name: Handsome Hero
runners.spotlight: Only sixth in his first trial of the year but has won his last two, latterly defying a slow start to win cosily; could feature.
runners.comment: Only sixth in his first trial of the year but has won his last two; could feature

races.race_name: The Hong Kong Reunification Cup (Handicap) (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Gorgeous Win
runners.spotlight: Nichola Yuen claims 10lb but he needs a turnaround in his form and was only sixth in a recent trial.
runners.comment: He needs a turnaround in his form and was only sixth in a recent trial

runners.name: Top Dragon
runners.spotlight: 3-14; perhaps unlucky not to win 1m Class 2 handicap here two runs ago and following defeat was over 1m2f; has to prove himself worthy of this high a mark but not dismissed; recent trial figures of 643 are not overly inspiring, however.
runners.comment: Bit to prove off this high a mark and recent trial figures of 643 are not overly inspiring
runners.owner: Vincent To Wai Keung, K To Kin Ting & R To Yiu Ting

runners.name: Packing Hermod
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who is comfortable at this level; highly tried behind Ka Ying Rising before struggling from a bad draw in a handicap here two months ago; only fifth and fourth in subsequent trials but dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner who is comfortable at this level; excuses in last two starts

runners.name: Emblazon
runners.spotlight: Some feat to win four consecutive handicaps (6f-7f) between December and March but his form has run cold in races over 1m2f, 7f and 6f; even if back on song this mark isn't easy.
runners.comment: Form has gone cold after a purple patch; even if back on song this mark isn't easy

runners.name: Regal Gem
runners.spotlight: Gained all five wins over 6f but only eighth over that far last time after contesting the Hong Kong Derby; others preferred.
runners.comment: All five wins over 6f but only eighth over that far last time after running in the Derby

runners.name: Storm Rider
runners.spotlight: One of three wins last season was a 7f Group 3 handicap; he can deal with this mark and latest run here over 6f was eyecatching.
runners.comment: He can deal with this mark and latest run here over 6f was eyecatching

runners.name: Beauty Bolt
runners.spotlight: 6f looked too sharp last time but he probably wants further than this far having done so well at 1m; he'll need a strong pace up front.
runners.comment: 6f too sharp last time but probably wants further than this far having done so well at 1m
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Public Attention
runners.spotlight: Classy form in Australia and back on track for his new yard with length defeats in 6f handicaps here; won't mind returning to 7f.
runners.comment: Back on track for his new yard with length defeats in 6f handicaps here; stays 7f

runners.name: Ka Ying Attack
runners.spotlight: Consistent 4yo with a strike-rate of 4-17 after winning over 6f here a month ago in good style; this is a rise in grade and he's 8lb higher, but should give his running; stays 7f; second in a recent trial.
runners.comment: This is a rise in grade and he's 8lb higher, but should give his running

runners.name: Invincible Shield
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who won over 6f here in January and has continued to run well at that trip, including when nearest at the finish in a Group 3 handicap a month ago; respected off the same mark now back over further.
runners.comment: C&D winner; closing at the finish in a 6f Group 3 handicap here the last time

races.race_name: Serving The Community Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Incanto Star
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; finished well for close fifth in 7f Class 5 event here ten days ago but is quickly back up in grade today and the drop back to 6f is not an obvious move.
runners.comment: Finished well for close fifth over 7f ten days ago; quickly back up in grade today

runners.name: Swagger Bro
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing on dirt here three months ago and not seen since but was placed on his latest turf start and has slipped to a tempting mark; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Absent since sixth on dirt three months ago but returns to turf on a tempting mark

runners.name: Silver Spurs
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in good nick when 48-1 second over C&D in May but was a disappointing favourite here last month and has another double-digit draw today; others look safer.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in form two starts ago but a disappointing favourite here last time

runners.name: Incredible Moment
runners.spotlight: Held on today's mark twice since winning readily from the front over C&D in early May but is better drawn this time and could give this a good shot.
runners.comment: Held off this mark twice since C&D win in May but better drawn to attack this time

runners.name: Romantic Laos
runners.spotlight: Reserve; regressive 7yo who has not won since December 2024; never better than midfield over 7f last month and this drop back in trip is not in his favour.
runners.comment: Never better than midfield over 7f last month and this drop back in trip is not ideal

runners.name: Storm Mirror
runners.spotlight: Unlucky not to finish closer than seventh on debut in May and kept on well for close third later that month; moves up from 5f today and could still have a lot more to offer; commands respect.
runners.comment: Close third over 5f here in May, his second run, and probably has more to offer over 6f

runners.name: Positive Smile
runners.spotlight: Ran on well for third over C&D two starts ago but 7f is his optimum trip, and he disappointed when last in action; others preferred.
runners.comment: 7f is his optimum trip and he disappointed when last in action; others preferred

runners.name: High Rise Victory
runners.spotlight: A well-beaten outsider on both starts (6f/7f); can't be recommended.
runners.comment: A well-beaten outsider on both starts (6f/7f); can't be recommended

runners.name: Run Run Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed twice over 5f since his breakthrough win over that trip in March but his form has dipped lately and he has a point to prove on this first 6f attempt.
runners.comment: In good form over 5f in early part of year but has a point to prove on first 6f attempt
runners.owner: Tang Ming, James Tang Sau Yip Et Al

runners.name: Bullish Nova
runners.spotlight: Began career with two promising 6f runs at Happy Valley, keeping on well to make the frame both times; will probably be dropped in again from his wide draw here; no surprise if he appears late on the scene.
runners.comment: Began career with two promising runs at Happy Valley; can appear late on the scene

runners.name: Glory B
runners.spotlight: Began season with two down-the-field runs in the autumn and returned from a break with another low-key effort last month; drawn wide; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Yet to strike form during light campaign this season and drawn wide today
runners.owner: Dr Bankee Kwan Pak Hoo & Jeffrey Kwan Teng Hin

runners.name: Stunning Bundle
runners.spotlight: Makes official debut after some underwhelming recent trials; favourable draw not enough to make him of interest.
runners.comment: Makes official debut after some underwhelming recent trials
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Colin Lo Chor Cheong

runners.name: Steadfast Fort
runners.spotlight: Out of a 6.5f winner; came second in a recent 6f dirt trial and evidently has some ability; makes official debut today.
runners.comment: Out of a 6.5f winner; came second in a recent 6f dirt trial; makes official debut today

runners.name: Green Field Pearl
runners.spotlight: Persistently denied clear run on debut here (5f) in February but posed no threat the following month; upped in trip for first run since; probably one for a bit further down the line.
runners.comment: Didn't show enough on his two 5f starts here in February/March

races.race_name: The Guangdong-Hong Kong Cup (Handicap) (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Mickley
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over 6f here in February/March and quite well handicapped on that form but hasn't been at the same level lately and needs to prove he's fully effective over 5f.
runners.comment: Not at best lately and seems better suited by 6f+

runners.name: Parents' Love
runners.spotlight: Exploited reduced in C&D Class 4 event in April but didn't run well here last month and his overall profile is not that appealing.
runners.comment: Class 4 C&D winner off reduced mark in April but overall profile not that appealing
runners.owner: Edmund Ho Kwok Shing, Joe Fong Fu Yuen Et Al

runners.name: Together We Value
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in Hong Kong but has been making good late headway on recent outings; each-way claims at least if he gets a good pace to aim at.
runners.comment: 0-7 in Hong Kong but has been making good late headway on recent outings
runners.owner: 25/26 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Metro Power
runners.spotlight: On fairy tough mark since narrow 6f win in March but recent 5f form has been respectable (cheekpieces refitted last time) and he's well drawn; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: On tough mark but recent form is respectable and he's well drawn; won't be far away

runners.name: Dragon Four Seas
runners.spotlight: Left Manfred Man after a string of disappointing efforts and there has been no upturn after three starts for current stable; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: In poor form this season, the last three times for this stable
runners.owner: Dr Alan Lee Wai Kin, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al

runners.name: Lucky Candy
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in Hong Kong but placed here on last four appearances, losing out only by a short head on latest occasion 24 days ago; ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Went very close over C&D last month and has now been placed four consecutive times
runners.owner: Yeung Wai Kwong & Lee Sheung Ping

runners.name: Dancing Classics
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who won twice in Class 4 last year (5f/6f); returned from layoff with low-key run last month but remains open to progress if that outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Dual winner in 2025; made low-key return in June but still has low mileage

runners.name: Red Elegance
runners.spotlight: Has a C&D win on his CV but is better known for his exploits at Happy Valley; out of form this season and needs a revival.
runners.comment: Out of form this season and needs a revival

runners.name: Cala Dei Mori
runners.spotlight: Listed 2yo winner when based in Italy but struggled on Hong Kong debut here (6f) one month ago.
runners.comment: Listed 2yo winner when based in Italy but struggled here on Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Watch Legend
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 3yo; well beaten over C&D on Hong Kong debut in May and needs to leave that performance a long way behind.
runners.comment: Well beaten over C&D on Hong Kong debut in May

runners.name: Greater Bae
runners.spotlight: Reserve; scored over 6f in January but has been below that form lately (blinkers added last time) and this drop to 5f is not sure to suit.
runners.comment: 6f winner in January but below that level lately; drop to 5f not sure to suit

runners.name: Master Of All
runners.spotlight: Gained all five wins over 7f, the latest on seasonal debut in September; not disgraced over C&D when dropped back in trip two starts ago but generally in poor form since December; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Generally in poor form since December and all five wins came over 7f

runners.name: Straight To Glory
runners.spotlight: Three wins this year, the latest over C&D in May; held off this mark since, though, and needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Three wins this season, the latest over C&D; needs to kick on again to overcome this mark
runners.owner: Chan Tim Shing & Pang Wai Hak

runners.name: Solid State
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-7 in Australia and absent since two handicap fourths (5.5f/6f) towards end of last year; probably best watched on Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: 0-7 in Australia and absent since two handicap fourths towards end of last year

runners.name: We Are Hero
runners.spotlight: Drops back to optimum trip after three down-the-field finishes over 6f/7f but is still 4lb higher than when forcing a dead-heat over C&D in March; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Back over optimum trip today but still 4lb higher than when dead-heating over C&D in March

runners.name: Fast Responder
runners.spotlight: Doubled Hong Kong tally over C&D in Apri, holding on by a neck under 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen, but was safely held on his latest outing and needs to rebound with a career-best effort.
runners.comment: Held of this mark since C&D win in April; career-best effort required
runners.owner: Hong Kong St John Presidents Club Syndicate

races.race_name: Continuous Development Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Power Patch
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on first four starts but his keeping-on fifth here (6f) last month was a step in the right direction; back up in trip today.
runners.comment: Improved form when keeping-on fifth here (6f) last month, his fifth start

runners.name: Cheerful Wongchoy
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all five starts (6f-7f); certainly not devoid of ability but has awkward draw today and will probably be of greater interesting when eligible for Class 5 races.
runners.comment: Not devoid of ability but will probably be of greater interest when dropped to Class 5

runners.name: Stormy Knight
runners.spotlight: Reserve; Unplaced all seven starts, most recently when never-dangerous seventh in C&D race won by Honourary last month.
runners.comment: Never dangerous when seventh over C&D last month and now 0-7
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs James Tien Pei Chun

runners.name: Strathpeffer
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing when last in action and not one to rely upon heavily but has posted some good efforts this season (including a C&D win) and his current mark is not beyond him.
runners.comment: Not one to rely upon heavily but has posted some good efforts over C&D this season

runners.name: Awesome Treasure
runners.spotlight: Badly out of form over 6f in recent months and this 59th career start with be his first venture beyond that trip.
